giant_rat.name: giant rat
giant_rat.lore: Giant rats infest the upper warrens of every dungeon, drawn by the smell of refuse and carrion. Their teeth can gnaw through wood and soft metals. While individually weak, they often swarm in packs and their bites can carry a mild fever.

goblin.name: goblin
goblin.lore: Goblins are small, cunning humanoids who lurk in the shallow tunnels near the dungeon's surface. They raid in packs, using numbers and traps to compensate for their lack of individual strength. Adventurers often find crude coins and stolen trinkets among their remains.

grid_bug.name: grid bug
grid_bug.lore: Grid bugs are bizarre insectoid creatures that can only move in straight lines along invisible magical lattices. Their origin is unknown — some sages believe they are fragments of a broken spell matrix given physical form. They carry nothing of value.

floating_eye.name: floating eye
floating_eye.lore: Floating eyes drift silently through dungeon corridors, their massive unblinking gaze capable of locking a creature in paralytic terror. They have no limbs or mouth, existing solely to observe. Sages believe they are the discarded sensors of some greater, unseen entity.

bat.name: bat
bat.lore: The giant bats of the deep cave networks are far more aggressive than their surface cousins. Their echolocation has evolved into a disorienting sonic pulse that scrambles the inner ear. Colonies roost in cavern ceilings, dropping on prey from above.

cobra.name: cobra
cobra.lore: Cave cobras have adapted to total darkness, developing pale scales and heat-sensing pits along their jawline. Their venom is more potent than their surface-dwelling kin. Alchemists prize cobra venom glands for antidote research.

zombie.name: zombie
zombie.lore: Zombies are the crudest form of undead — corpses reanimated by ambient necromantic energy or a careless spell. They shuffle toward the living with single-minded hunger, spreading disease through festering wounds. The disease they carry is believed to originate from the Plane of Rot.

gelatinous_cube.name: gelatinous cube
gelatinous_cube.lore: Gelatinous cubes are semi-transparent oozes perfectly sized to fill dungeon corridors, acting as natural cleaning agents. They engulf and dissolve all organic matter, while indigestible items — coins, gems, weapons — remain suspended within their bodies. They are nearly invisible until they have already engulfed you.

wraith.name: wraith
wraith.lore: Wraiths are the spirits of the deeply corrupt, those whose evil was so profound that death could not contain them. They drain life force with a touch, leaving victims pale and weakened. Unlike ghosts, wraiths have no memory of their former lives — only an insatiable hunger for vitality.

mimic.name: mimic
mimic.lore: Mimics are shapeshifting predators that disguise themselves as treasure chests, doors, or dungeon furnishings. Their digestive acid can dissolve iron. The oldest mimics have survived for centuries by perfecting their camouflage, accumulating wealth from victims who mistook them for a lucky find.

leprechaun.name: leprechaun
leprechaun.lore: Leprechauns are trickster fey who wander dungeons collecting gold. Their hexes scramble spatial perception, sending pursuers stumbling in random directions. They carry considerable wealth but spend more energy protecting it with illusions and curses than an honest warrior might spend on a sword.

kobold.name: kobold
kobold.lore: Kobolds are small draconic humanoids who worship dragons and mine the deep earth. What they lack in strength they compensate with elaborate traps and ambushes. Kobold warrens are riddled with pit traps, tripwires, and poisoned darts. Their obsession with dragon hoards makes them surprisingly good miners.

cave_spider.name: cave spider
cave_spider.lore: Cave spiders spin invisible webs across dungeon passages, waiting motionless for days until prey stumbles through. Their venom causes progressive paralysis rather than immediate death — they prefer to consume prey alive. The silk they produce is of remarkable tensile strength.

skeleton.name: skeleton
skeleton.lore: Animated by residual necromantic magic, skeletons are the most common undead in the shallow dungeon levels. They retain no memory or personality, fighting until destroyed. Piercing weapons slip between their ribs, but slashing blows can shatter their bones.

fire_beetle.name: fire beetle
fire_beetle.lore: Fire beetles store bioluminescent fuel in special glands behind their eyes and at their abdomen. In the wild, this light attracts mates; in the dungeon, they have evolved to project it defensively as a short-range ignition burst. Their glands remain potent for days after death.

orc_scout.name: orc scout
orc_scout.lore: Orc scouts are the vanguard of larger warbands, sent ahead to map territory and eliminate weak prey. They travel light, relying on speed and surprise rather than heavy armor. Their tribal tattoos record their kills — a scout with many markings is especially dangerous.

gnoll.name: gnoll
gnoll.lore: Gnolls are hyena-headed humanoids who live to hunt and kill. Unlike orcs, they do not build settlements — they follow prey until it collapses, consuming everything including bone. Their laughter-like vocalizations signal a pack closing in for the kill.

hobgoblin.name: hobgoblin
hobgoblin.lore: Hobgoblins are the military aristocracy of the goblinoid races, disciplined and organized where their lesser kin are chaotic. They train in formation tactics and maintain strict hierarchies. A hobgoblin warband is a genuine military threat, capable of holding dungeon territory against much larger monsters.

ghoul.name: ghoul
ghoul.lore: Ghouls were once people — usually those who resorted to cannibalism in extremity. The curse of the ghoul transforms them fully, leaving them with an insatiable appetite for humanoid flesh. Their claws secrete a paralytic enzyme, allowing them to feed at leisure. They hoard the jewelry of victims in their lairs.

dire_rat.name: dire rat
dire_rat.lore: Dire rats are the size of large dogs and far more aggressive than ordinary rats. Pack hunters, they coordinate attacks to overwhelm larger prey. Their teeth can sever fingers, and their bites often fester due to their filthy habits.

acid_blob.name: acid blob
acid_blob.lore: Acid blobs are semi-intelligent oozes that secrete a powerful corrosive that dissolves metal and bone with equal efficiency. They leave trails of etched stone wherever they travel. Items found within a dead acid blob's mass are typically damaged but still recoverable.

troll.name: troll
troll.lore: Trolls are enormously resilient creatures whose flesh regenerates wounds almost as fast as they are inflicted. Only fire and acid can prevent regrowth. Dungeon trolls are larger and more aggressive than their surface kin, evolved in a world of constant violent competition. They hoard shiny objects without understanding their value.

orc_warrior.name: orc warrior
orc_warrior.lore: Orc warriors are the backbone of orcish warbands, equipped with salvaged armor and brutal weapons. They fight with reckless aggression, bolstered by a cultural belief that dying in battle earns favor with their war-god. A cornered orc warrior fights with increased ferocity.

dark_elf.name: dark elf
dark_elf.lore: Dark elves — drow — are exiles from the surface world who have built a civilization in the deepest tunnels. Their magic is potent but their iron corrodes rapidly in surface air. Drow who venture into the upper dungeon are typically scouts, assassins, or outcasts from even their own cruel society.

wight.name: wight
wight.lore: Wights are powerful undead who retain some semblance of their former intelligence, making them more dangerous than mindless skeletons. They dwell in ancient burial mounds and ruined fortresses, commanding lesser undead. Their touch drains life force, and those slain by a wight often rise as undead themselves.

rust_monster.name: rust monster
rust_monster.lore: Rust monsters are the terror of armored adventurers — their antenna touch causes ferrous metal to instantly corrode into worthless flakes. They are not aggressive by nature, seeking metal to consume rather than flesh. A dungeon where rust monsters dwell is stripped of iron fixtures.

cave_bear.name: cave bear
cave_bear.lore: Cave bears are massive omnivores that dominate middle dungeon levels the way brown bears dominate forests. Enormously strong and highly territorial, they attack with a devastating combination of claws and bite. Their hides are prized by tanners for exceptional thickness and warmth.

ogre.name: ogre
ogre.lore: Ogres are brutish giants standing eight feet tall, motivated primarily by hunger and the desire to smash things. Despite their dim intellect, they are cunning enough to set ambushes and use improvised weapons effectively. Ogre warrens are identified by the smell — they hoard food alongside stolen goods.

shadow.name: shadow
shadow.lore: Shadows are incorporeal undead that drain strength from living creatures, growing more solid with each point of ability they steal. They lurk in dark corners and beneath furniture, indistinguishable from ordinary shadows until they move. A person drained to zero Strength by a shadow rises as a shadow themselves.

mummy.name: mummy
mummy.lore: Mummies are the preserved dead of ancient kings and priests, animated to guard tomb treasures for eternity. Their rotted bandages carry a supernatural disease — mummy rot — that resists natural healing. The curse they carry is linked to the original embalming rites; defeating the mummy that placed it can lift the curse.

giant_spider.name: giant spider
giant_spider.lore: Giant spiders of the deep dungeon grow to the size of horses, spinning massive orb webs that span entire corridors. Their venom is fast-acting and powerful. Victims they do not immediately consume are wrapped in silk and stored for later — the cocoons often contain items from previous adventurers.

ettin.name: ettin
ettin.lore: Ettins are two-headed giants whose dual consciousnesses argue constantly about everything — including combat tactics. This gives them the ability to attack and defend simultaneously, never truly caught off-guard. Their lairs are littered with the remains of meals and the occasional intact trophy from a memorable fight.

manticore.name: manticore
manticore.lore: Manticores have the body of a lion, the wings of a dragon, and a barbed tail that launches volleys of spikes with deadly precision. Persian scholars describe them as the perfect predator, a creature that can fight at range or close quarters equally well. They relish the taste of human flesh above all other prey.

specter.name: specter
specter.lore: Specters are more powerful than wraiths, incorporeal undead who have burned away all remnants of their former personality in their hunger for life force. They can pass through solid stone. The dread cold that precedes a specter's arrival lowers ambient temperature by several degrees — a useful warning sign.

basilisk.name: basilisk
basilisk.lore: The basilisk's eight-eyed gaze carries petrification magic — sustained eye contact will turn flesh to stone within seconds. The dungeon basilisk is smaller than the legendary surface variety but equally deadly. Their stone-form victims litter their lairs, sometimes still in postures of final surprise.

chimera.name: chimera
chimera.lore: The chimera is a magical aberration with the body of a lion, the head of a goat, and a serpentine tail. Its goat head breathes fire, its lion head bites, and its tail delivers venomous strikes. Ancient texts describe chimeras as failed divine experiments — amalgams of multiple beasts fused by careless magic.

minotaur.name: minotaur
minotaur.lore: Minotaurs are the cursed offspring of divine punishment, possessing a bull's head and enormous strength in a humanoid body. They possess perfect spatial memory, never becoming lost in labyrinths they know. Dungeon minotaurs are often solitary, claiming entire sections of dungeon as their territory and marking passages with the remains of intruders.

frost_crawler.name: frost crawler
frost_crawler.lore: Frost crawlers are centipede-like creatures from the frozen sub-levels, their exoskeleton covered in natural ice crystals. They secrete a supercooled slime that flash-freezes flesh on contact. Their lair temperatures drop noticeably below ambient — dungeon explorers use this cold draft as a warning.

medusa.name: medusa
medusa.lore: The medusa is a serpent-haired horror whose gaze turns flesh to stone. Greek myth held that only one existed, but dungeon scholars have catalogued several varieties. They are cunning and use their collection of stone victims as both decoration and tactical concealment. Blind adventurers are immune to their primary ability.

vampire_spawn.name: vampire spawn
vampire_spawn.lore: Vampire spawn are newly turned vampires who have not yet mastered their powers. They serve their sire as thralls, hunting on command. Unlike true vampires they cannot transform into mist or animals. Their drain attack is weaker than a full vampire's but they are more numerous and easier to encounter.

fire_elemental.name: fire elemental
fire_elemental.lore: Fire elementals are fragments of the Elemental Plane of Fire summoned or drawn through planar weak points in volcanic dungeon sections. They burn with intense heat that ignites everything they touch. They cannot enter water and are confused by cold spells that partially extinguish their flames.

earth_elemental.name: earth elemental
earth_elemental.lore: Earth elementals rise from dungeon floors as walking masses of stone and compressed soil. They move through rock as easily as a fish through water, ambushing from below. Their immense weight makes them devastating in close combat but slow to pursue a fleeing target across open ground.

storm_elemental.name: storm elemental
storm_elemental.lore: Storm elementals crackle with contained lightning and howling winds, drawn to the deep dungeon by resonant geomantic currents. Their strikes discharge static electricity that chains between metal objects. Metal armor actually increases one's vulnerability to their attacks — a fact armored adventurers learn painfully.

lich_apprentice.name: lich apprentice
lich_apprentice.lore: A lich apprentice is a mage who has begun the terrible transformation into lichdom but has not completed the phylactery ritual. They retain enough life to feel pain but enough undeath to ignore most of it. They serve more powerful liches in exchange for the secret of immortality, a secret their master has no intention of sharing.

mind_flayer.name: mind flayer
mind_flayer.lore: Mind flayers are tentacled horror from the deep places, sustained entirely by consuming the brains of sentient beings. Their psychic blast can scramble thought itself. An ancient mind flayer city is said to exist in the deepest dungeon levels, its architecture built from the calcified remains of thousands of victims.

vampire.name: vampire
vampire.lore: True vampires are among the most dangerous undead — immortal predators who have refined their hunting over centuries. They can transform into mist, command wolves and bats, and regenerate damage rapidly outside of sunlight. Each vampire has a personality and agenda; most are collecting power as obsessively as they once collected gold.

death_knight.name: death knight
death_knight.lore: Death knights are the accursed remains of paladins who fell from grace so completely that even death rejected them. They retain their martial skill and gain dark magic, forced to serve whatever dark power claimed their soul. Their hellfire swords are a mark of their eternal damnation — they cannot remove them.

bone_golem.name: bone golem
bone_golem.lore: Bone golems are constructs assembled from the skeletons of multiple creatures, held together by necromantic binding. Unlike undead, they have no animus of their own — they execute commands mechanically. Their disjointed anatomy makes them eerily resistant to cutting weapons; the bones simply separate and reconnect.

greater_demon.name: greater demon
greater_demon.lore: Greater demons are powerful entities of the Abyss, drawn to the material plane by large-scale evil or summoning rituals. They are not merely powerful — they are willfully cruel, seeking to corrupt and destroy with equal enthusiasm. Their very presence twists reality; flowers wilt and children cry at their passing.

young_dragon.name: young dragon
young_dragon.lore: Young dragons are already more dangerous than most dungeon denizens despite not yet reaching their full potential. They collect treasure obsessively, a drive that begins in youth and never diminishes. A young dragon's hoard is modest compared to an adult's but still represents considerable wealth accumulated through violence.

beholder.name: beholder
beholder.lore: Beholders are aberrations of alien intelligence, their spherical bodies covered in eyestalks each projecting a different magical ray. They are deeply paranoid, trusting no one — not even others of their own kind. Beholder lairs contain the petrified, charmed, or disintegrated remains of everyone they have ever met.

vampire_lord.name: vampire lord
vampire_lord.lore: A vampire lord has survived for a millennium or more, accumulating power, wealth, and influence across the mortal world. They control vast networks of thralls and spawn, and their castle or lair typically occupies an entire dungeon level. They regard adventurers as momentary amusements or potential additions to their collection.

lich.name: lich
lich.lore: A lich is a wizard who achieved immortality through a dark ritual, binding their soul to a phylactery. They cannot be permanently destroyed while the phylactery survives. Centuries of study have made liches extraordinarily powerful; decades of undeath have stripped away their humanity, leaving only intellect and ambition.

adult_dragon.name: adult dragon
adult_dragon.lore: Adult dragons are calculating predators who have lived long enough to understand strategy, politics, and the value of reputation. Their hoard is not merely accumulated wealth but a carefully curated collection representing centuries of domination. They often know exactly what every item in their hoard is and how it was taken.

iron_golem.name: iron golem
iron_golem.lore: Iron golems are the masterwork of the golem-maker's art — eight feet of animated metal, immune to fire, magic, and most physical harm. They breathe toxic gas from their forge-heated interiors. Built to guard powerful mages' vaults, they continue their work long after their creators have died of old age.

greater_lich.name: greater lich
greater_lich.lore: A greater lich has transcended even the elevated power of standard liches, their magical ability grown so immense that reality bends in their presence. They have had centuries to perfect their phylactery's hiding place. Many have become interested in philosophical or arcane problems too abstract for mortal minds to comprehend.

arch_demon.name: arch demon
arch_demon.lore: An arch-demon rules a layer of the Abyss, commanding millions of lesser demons. When one descends to the material plane, it is fulfilling a plan centuries in the making. They are nearly impossible to banish without knowledge of their true name, which they guard with obsessive care.

ancient_dragon.name: ancient dragon
ancient_dragon.lore: Ancient dragons are among the oldest intelligent beings on the material plane. They have watched kingdoms rise and fall, and their accumulated knowledge rivals any library. Their hoard contains items of historical significance as much as monetary value. Meeting one is either the greatest opportunity or greatest danger an adventurer will ever face.

ancient_lich.name: ancient lich
ancient_lich.lore: An ancient lich has persisted for thousands of years, their original mortal identity long since dissolved in millennia of undead existence. They have achieved magical understanding that makes even other liches look like apprentices. Their phylactery is typically hidden in a demiplane of their own creation.

death_lord.name: death lord
death_lord.lore: Death lords are the apex of the undead hierarchy — beings who have transcended individual undeath to embody the concept of death itself. They command the obedience of all lesser undead within miles of their presence. Ancient texts describe them as heralds of an End that has not yet come.

chaos_spawn.name: chaos spawn
chaos_spawn.lore: Chaos spawns are the physical manifestation of raw entropic energy, their form shifting constantly between states. No two look alike and they change even as you fight them. Philosophers debate whether chaos spawns have consciousness or are simply self-organizing patterns of disorder given temporary solidity.

soul_eater.name: soul eater
soul_eater.lore: Soul eaters are entities from a plane adjacent to death, sustained by consuming the animating essence of living beings. A creature whose soul is devoured cannot be resurrected by ordinary means. They are drawn to high concentrations of life force — adventurers deep in dungeons represent exceptionally attractive prey.

orc_shaman.name: orc shaman
orc_shaman.lore: Orc shamans channel the dark blessings of their tribal deity, bolstering warband members and hurling curses at enemies. They are respected and feared within orcish society — only the warchief has more authority. Their ritual tattoos glow with eldritch energy during combat.

giant_centipede.name: giant centipede
giant_centipede.lore: Giant centipedes are common throughout the dungeon's middle levels, their hundreds of legs moving with disturbing speed. Their venom causes intense burning pain that disrupts concentration. Alchemists use centipede venom in paralytic preparations, but extracting it from a living specimen is extremely dangerous.

skeletal_archer.name: skeletal archer
skeletal_archer.lore: Skeletal archers are the reanimated remains of warriors who were skilled with the bow in life. The necromantic animation preserves their martial memory, allowing them to draw and fire with mechanical precision. They prefer to maintain distance, riddling targets with arrows before retreating behind their melee companions.

cave_toad.name: cave toad
cave_toad.lore: Cave toads have evolved in the absolute darkness of underground lakes and flooded passages. Their swollen glands secrete a powerful hallucinogen as a defensive measure. Dungeon explorers who mistake them for food sources experience vivid, sometimes permanent, sensory alterations.

brigand.name: brigand
brigand.lore: Brigands are former soldiers, escaped criminals, or simply desperate people who have turned to dungeon raiding and banditry. Unlike monsters, they have actual combat training and equipment. Some brigand bands have claimed entire dungeon sections as territory, extorting passing adventurers or simply killing them.

shadow_hound.name: shadow hound
shadow_hound.lore: Shadow hounds are canine predators partially displaced into the Shadow Plane, their bodies semi-transparent and cold to the touch. They can step through solid objects briefly to bypass obstacles or flank prey. Their howl causes existential dread — adventurers who hear it often report feeling suddenly alone in all the universe.

flesh_golem.name: flesh golem
flesh_golem.lore: Flesh golems are patchwork constructs assembled from the bodies of multiple humanoids, animated by lightning and necromantic science. The creator's manual warns that a flesh golem occasionally rebels if its animating intelligence coalesces into something resembling grief. Their berserk episodes are legendary among golem artificers.

harpy.name: harpy
harpy.lore: Harpies are winged women-creatures from Greek myth, their lower bodies those of great birds of prey. Their enchanting song lures victims to walk heedlessly toward danger. Despite their magical ability, they are filthy and cruel by nature — their nesting sites are identifiable by smell alone.

necromancer_apprentice.name: necromancer apprentice
necromancer_apprentice.lore: A necromancer's apprentice has learned enough dark magic to animate lesser undead and hurl bolts of necrotic energy, but has not yet achieved the mastery to safely attempt lichdom. They typically follow a senior necromancer, performing the gruesome preparatory work their master finds beneath their dignity.

stymphalian_bird.name: Stymphalian Bird
stymphalian_bird.lore: Ares' sacred birds of Lake Stymphalia bore bronze beaks, claws, and feathers they launched like arrows. Heracles drove them away with Hephaestus' bronze krotala. In these dungeons they roost in high crevices and dive at unwary explorers.

satyr_warrior.name: Satyr Warrior
satyr_warrior.lore: Satyrs are the rowdy half-goat companions of Dionysus, known more for wine and revelry than war. Those who take up arms are exiles cast out from their bands, bitter and violent. They fight with crude clubs and use their horned heads to disorient opponents.

empusa.name: Empusa
empusa.lore: Empusai are shape-shifting demonesses sent by Hecate to devour travelers at crossroads. They can appear as beautiful women, dogs, or cows before revealing their true form with one leg of bronze and one of an ass. They drain the life force of their victims through seductive illusions.

hecate_hound.name: Hecate's Hound
hecate_hound.lore: Hecate, goddess of magic and crossroads, keeps spectral hounds that patrol the boundaries between the living world and the underworld. Their mournful howls can shatter a mortal's concentration and drive them to madness. They are encountered most frequently near dungeon stairwells.

lamia.name: Lamia
lamia.lore: Born beautiful and cursed by Hera to devour children after her own were slain, Lamia wanders the deep places half-woman half-serpent, stealing life and warmth from the living.

gorgon_warrior.name: Gorgon Warrior
gorgon_warrior.lore: Not to be confused with Medusa's sisters, the Gorgon warriors are bull-like bronze creatures whose breath can petrify living flesh. Ancient sources describe them as cattle possessed by Ares whose exhalations turn enemies to stone. In the dungeon they patrol as living armored sentinels.

cyclops.name: Cyclops
cyclops.lore: The Cyclopes are one-eyed giants, sons of Poseidon, who dwell in caves and scorn civilization. Polyphemus, most famous of their kind, trapped Odysseus's crew until the hero blinded him with a sharpened stake. Their single eye grants unusual depth perception for throwing boulders but leaves them vulnerable to flanking.

sphinx.name: Sphinx
sphinx.lore: The Sphinx crouched at the gates of Thebes and devoured all who could not answer her riddle: what walks on four legs at dawn, two at noon, three at dusk? Only Oedipus answered correctly and she threw herself from her rock. In the dungeon she sits at crossroads, guarding passage, and will attack any who approach without first attempting her puzzle.

griffin.name: Griffin
griffin.lore: Griffins, with the body of a lion and the head and wings of an eagle, are ancient guardians of divine gold in the far north. Sacred to Apollo and Nemesis, they mate for life and aggressively defend their nests. They are prized by kings for their feathers, which are said to change color in the presence of poison.

pegasus.name: Pegasus (Hostile)
pegasus.lore: Born from the blood of Medusa when Perseus struck her down, Pegasus is the divine winged horse associated with thunder and the Muses. While normally gentle when approached by heroes, dungeon-corrupted Pegasi have been driven mad by confinement underground and charge anything that moves.

hydra.name: Lernaean Hydra
hydra.lore: The Hydra of Lerna, a water serpent with nine heads, was one of Heracles' twelve labors. Whenever a head was severed, two more grew in its place — until Iolaus cauterized each stump with fire. The dungeon variant still regenerates if not dealt with carefully, and its blood is so toxic that Heracles used it to poison his arrows.

cerberus.name: Cerberus
cerberus.lore: The three-headed hound of Hades guards the entrance to the underworld, permitting the dead to enter but none to leave. Heracles subdued him through sheer strength for his twelfth labor; Orpheus charmed all three heads to sleep with his lyre. In the deepest dungeon he serves as an eternal guardian, his serpent-barbed tail lashing at any who attempt to pass.

scylla.name: Scylla
scylla.lore: Once a beautiful sea nymph, Scylla was transformed by Circe's jealousy into a twelve-legged monster with six heads, each bearing three rows of teeth. She occupies a narrow sea-cave and snatches sailors from passing ships — even from Odysseus's crew. In the dungeon she anchors herself in a narrow passage and attacks all who try to pass.

charybdis_spawn.name: Charybdis Spawn
charybdis_spawn.lore: Charybdis, the great whirlpool monster, is said to be a daughter of Poseidon who was struck by Zeus's thunderbolt and cast into the sea. Three times daily she swallows the sea and three times regurgitates it. Her spawn are smaller vortex-creatures that cling to dungeon walls and drag creatures toward their central maw.

titanspawn.name: Titanspawn
titanspawn.lore: The Titans, elder gods overthrown by Zeus, were imprisoned in Tartarus beneath the deepest earth. Titanspawn are fragments of their primordial essence that have seeped upward through the dungeon rock — barely conscious but seething with ancient rage. They take vaguely humanoid form from the stone around them and attack everything that is not Titan-born.

frost_giant.name: Frost Giant
frost_giant.lore: Frost Giants, or Hrímþursar, dwell in Niflheim and Jötunheimr, ancient enemies of the Aesir gods. They are descendants of Ymir, the primordial being whose body Odin used to create the world. In Ragnarök they will march against Asgard — until then, they lurk in deep cold places, nursing ancient grievances.

fire_giant.name: Fire Giant
fire_giant.lore: Surtr, the lord of Múspellsheim, leads the Fire Giants — ancient beings of primordial flame who will burn the world at Ragnarök. They forge weapons in the heat of creation itself and are immune to all fire. Their kingdom lies south of the primordial void, and it is said Surtr's sword burns brighter than the sun.

valkyrie_shade.name: Valkyrie Shade
valkyrie_shade.lore: Valkyries are Odin's battle-maidens who choose the slain and carry the worthy to Valhalla. A Valkyrie Shade is the echo of one who failed in her divine duty — perhaps allowing an unworthy soul to enter Valhalla, or falling in battle herself. Stripped of Odin's blessing, they haunt the battlefield-like dungeon corridors, dragging living souls downward.

fenrir_pup.name: Fenrir's Pup
fenrir_pup.lore: Fenrir is the monstrous wolf son of Loki, bound by the dwarven ribbon Gleipnir until Ragnarök. His offspring, Fenrir's Pups, are already of unnatural size and ferocity. They have inherited some of their father's essence — even Tyr, who fed Fenrir by hand, lost his right arm to the great wolf's jaws.

jormungandr_spawn.name: Jörmungandr Spawn
jormungandr_spawn.lore: Jörmungandr, the Midgard Serpent, encircles the entire world biting its own tail. It is Thor's destined nemesis — at Ragnarök they will slay each other. Its spawn are fragments of its vast coiled body, imbued with the world-poison that will kill even the god of thunder. They are encountered near underground rivers.

draugr.name: Draugr
draugr.lore: Draugr are the restless Norse undead who rise from burial mounds to protect their grave-goods. They can increase in size, pass through walls, and smell blood from great distances. Unlike simple zombies, draugr retain intelligence and malice, sometimes even the memories of their living selves — making them cunning opponents.

einherjar_ghost.name: Einherjar Ghost
einherjar_ghost.lore: The Einherjar are Odin's honored dead, warriors chosen from the battlefield to feast and train in Valhalla until Ragnarök. When an Einherjar ghost appears in the dungeon, it is one whose Valhalla was somehow denied — a warrior who died without witnesses, or whose funeral rites were incomplete. They fight with the skill of lifetimes but the despair of the twice-lost.

nidhogg_brood.name: Níðhöggr Brood
nidhogg_brood.lore: Níðhöggr is the great corpse-gnawing dragon who dwells at the root of Yggdrasil, gnawing the World Tree from below. Its brood are smaller wyrms that carry their parent's essence, infected with the same cosmic rot. They are found in the deepest passages, continuing their parent's slow work of destroying the foundations of the world.

hugin_raven.name: Huginn Raven
hugin_raven.lore: Huginn (Thought) and Muninn (Memory) are Odin's two ravens who fly across the world each day and report what they have seen. Dungeon ravens descended from this line carry a fragment of divine observation — they will flee rather than fight directly, but their pecking attacks aim unerringly for the eyes.

lindworm.name: Lindworm
lindworm.lore: The Lindworm is a wingless Norse dragon-serpent, vast and venomous. In legend, a prince was cursed to lindworm form and only freed when a bride willingly shed seven skins to match his own. Dungeon lindworms have no such curse to break — they lurk in flooded tunnels and strike with terrifying speed for their size.

berserker_spirit.name: Berserker Spirit
berserker_spirit.lore: Norse berserkers were warriors who entered a battle trance, fighting with the ferocity of wolves or bears, immune to pain and fear. The berserker spirit is the rage itself — a spectral warrior who never stopped fighting, long after the body died. They attack in a perpetual frenzy and their raw violence can unsettle even seasoned fighters.

light_elf_hostile.name: Corrupted Light Elf
light_elf_hostile.lore: Light Elves normally dwell in the radiant realm of Álfheimr, fairer than the sun. These have been corrupted — perhaps by Loki's influence or by contact with the dungeon's dark energies — and their once-benevolent light has curdled into a burning, indiscriminate radiance that sears all it touches.

sea_serpent_norse.name: Norse Sea Serpent
sea_serpent_norse.lore: Norse seas are home to immense serpentine creatures second only to Jörmungandr itself. Viking mariners recorded encounters with creatures long enough to encircle entire longships. These dungeon variants have followed underground rivers to their sources, coiling in flooded chambers, their scales bright with cold saltwater light.

anubis_guardian.name: Anubis Guardian
anubis_guardian.lore: Anubis is the jackal-headed god of embalming and guardian of the dead who weighs the heart against the feather of Ma'at. His guardians are divine soldiers who protect sacred burial sites from desecration. They carry crooks and flails — the symbols of judgment — and can pass judgment on a living soul, temporarily stopping the heart.

ra_serpent.name: Ra's Serpent
ra_serpent.lore: During the nightly solar barque journey, Ra faces Apep — the chaos serpent — in the underworld. Ra's own sacred serpents serve as guardians and weapons against Apep's minions. These golden serpents carry a fragment of solar fire in their venom and are blinding to behold when they strike.

scarab_swarm.name: Scarab Swarm
scarab_swarm.lore: The scarab beetle was sacred to Khepri, the dawn aspect of Ra, symbolizing rebirth and transformation. Corrupted scarab swarms have been twisted by tomb magic into ravenous hordes that strip flesh from bone. Individual beetles are trivial; thousands working together are terrifying. They pour through cracks in dungeon walls.

sphinx_guardian.name: Egyptian Sphinx
sphinx_guardian.lore: The Great Sphinx of Giza guards the plateau of Giza and, by extension, the Pharaoh's eternal rest. Egyptian sphinxes differ from their Greek cousins — they are pure guardians, manifestations of royal power and divine protection, without the Greek variant's penchant for fatal riddles. They will not parley. They destroy all who approach unbidden.

ammit_spawn.name: Ammit Spawn
ammit_spawn.lore: Ammit is the 'Devourer of the Dead,' a composite beast with the head of a crocodile, the forequarters of a lion, and the hindquarters of a hippopotamus. She waits beside the scales of Ma'at to consume hearts found unworthy — those she devours face the second death, true annihilation. Her spawn hunger for the living soul specifically.

sobek_warrior.name: Sobek Warrior
sobek_warrior.lore: Sobek, the crocodile god of the Nile, represents military prowess, fertility, and pharaonic power. His warriors are crocodile-headed humanoids armed with the curved khopesh sword, elite soldiers of his divine temple guard. They are notorious for the 'death roll' — dragging enemies to the ground and spinning until they stop resisting.

ba_spirit.name: Ba Spirit
ba_spirit.lore: The Ba is one of the Egyptian soul's components — the personality aspect, often depicted as a human-headed bird. When a burial is improper or the deceased's name is erased, the Ba cannot complete its journey and wanders, confused and hungry. In the dungeon, lost Ba spirits feed on the life force of the living.

khepri_beetle.name: Khepri Beetle
khepri_beetle.lore: Khepri is the scarab-headed god who rolls the sun across the sky each morning, a symbol of rebirth and transformation. His sacred beetles have grown to the size of large dogs in the dungeon's magical energies. They carry a solar charge in their carapaces that can release a blinding burst when threatened.

thoth_construct.name: Thoth Construct
thoth_construct.lore: Thoth, the ibis-headed god of knowledge, writing, and magic, created the divine scripts that govern reality. His constructs are animated guardians built of living hieroglyphics — each symbol on their body an active spell. They enforce the laws of Ma'at and attack those who carry forbidden knowledge or stolen divine artifacts.

duat_shade.name: Duat Shade
duat_shade.lore: The Duat is the Egyptian underworld through which the solar barque of Ra passes each night. Its shades are the fragments of souls that did not survive the weighing of the heart — not devoured by Ammit, but simply dissolved in the dark waters, too thin to be collected. They drift through the dungeon like smoke, hungry for the substance of the living.

desert_wraith.name: Desert Wraith
desert_wraith.lore: The Egyptian desert, the Red Land, was the domain of Set — a place of chaos, death, and transformation. Desert wraiths are the spirits of those who died lost in the wastes without burial: their bodies mummified by heat and wind, their souls unable to reach the Duat. They hunger to draw water from the living, desiccating their victims.

banshee.name: Banshee
banshee.lore: The Banshee (Bean Sídhe) is a fairy woman from Irish mythology whose wail presages the death of a family member. She appears as an old woman or a beautiful young woman with long pale hair, weeping and keening. In the dungeon, her wail is not a prophecy but a weapon — the sound alone can stop a mortal heart.

fomorian.name: Fomorian
fomorian.lore: The Fomorians are primordial beings from Irish mythology, representing the forces of chaos, darkness, and winter. They warred against the Tuatha Dé Danann in a series of cosmic battles. Fomorians often have single limbs or eyes placed centrally on the forehead — the evil eye of Balor could kill an army with a glance.

changeling.name: Changeling
changeling.lore: Changelings are fairy children left in place of stolen human infants, or fairy glamours shaped to mimic humans. They are always slightly wrong — too cold, too light, with eyes that reflect what should not be there. In the dungeon they appear as other monsters or adventurers until attacked, then reveal their true shifting, half-formed nature.

each_uisce.name: Each Uisce
each_uisce.lore: The Each Uisce, or Water Horse, is a supernatural sea-horse from Scottish and Irish folklore. More dangerous than the kelpie, it lurks in sea-lochs and deep coastal waters. Anyone who mounts it is carried beneath the waves and drowned, the rider's body often found minus the liver. In flooded dungeon passages, it appears as an unusually beautiful horse.

cath_palug.name: Cath Palug
cath_palug.lore: Cath Palug, the Clawing Cat, is a monstrous cat from Welsh and Arthurian mythology that devastated Anglesey before being fought by Kay himself. Some traditions say it was a divine cat, others that it was a monster nurtured by the children of Palug. In any form it is terrifyingly swift and its magical claws can rend spiritual armor.

fionn_hound.name: Fionn's Hound
fionn_hound.lore: Finn mac Cumhaill kept the two greatest hounds in Ireland — Bran and Sceolán, who were actually his nephews transformed by magic. Fionn's hounds are supernatural hunting dogs bred from this same divine lineage. Loyal to their master to the death, they have been turned against intruders in the dungeon and pursue relentlessly once they have a scent.

puca.name: Púca
puca.lore: The Púca is a shape-shifting Irish fairy that most often takes the form of a dark horse with glowing eyes, a goat, a rabbit, or a goblin. It leads travelers astray, sometimes offering rides that end dangerously. In the dungeon, a Púca serves as both obstacle and trickster — it may flee only to lead pursuers into worse danger.

dullahan_spawn.name: Dullahan Spawn
dullahan_spawn.lore: The Dullahan is the Irish headless horseman who rides a black horse and carries his head under his arm, using a spine as a whip. When he calls your name, you die. A Dullahan Spawn is one who has not yet found the name they seek — they wander dungeons looking for the soul that matches the name already on their lips.

sluagh.name: Sluagh
sluagh.lore: The Sluagh are the unforgiven dead in Irish mythology — souls so wicked that neither heaven nor earth would accept them, condemned to fly in dark swarms from the west. They are the most dangerous of the fairy hosts, seeking to carry off the souls of the dying from western windows. In the dungeon they descend in a cloud, tearing at any life they can reach.

pazuzu_spawn.name: Pazuzu Spawn
pazuzu_spawn.lore: Pazuzu is the Assyrian king of wind demons, lord of the southwest wind that brought drought and locusts. He is paradoxically used as a protective demon against other evil spirits, particularly Lamashtu who threatened pregnant women. His spawn carry his four wings, scorpion tail, and the feverish disease winds of the desert storm.

lahamu.name: Lahamu
lahamu.lore: Lahamu is one of the primordial beings of Babylonian mythology, born from the mingling of Apsu (fresh water) and Tiamat (salt water) before the world was fully formed. She and her consort Lahmu are among the eldest of deities. In the dungeon she manifests as a vast, undefined shape that seems to shift between snake and lion-headed woman.

anzu_bird.name: Anzu Bird
anzu_bird.lore: Anzu (also called Zu) is the great Sumerian lion-headed eagle who stole the Tablet of Destinies from the god Enlil, gaining control over fate itself. He was slain by Ninurta in a thunderstorm battle. The Anzu Bird in the dungeon breathes lightning as Ninurta threw thunderbolts — and carries the residual power of the stolen Tablet.

ugallu_demon.name: Ugallu Demon
ugallu_demon.lore: The Ugallu, or 'Great Weather Beast,' is a Mesopotamian demon with the head of a lion, the body of a man, and the feet of a bird. They are the servants of Tiamat, waging cosmic war against the Anunnaki. Their lion heads can emit a roar that disrupts spellcasting and their claws carry the chaos of the primordial waters.

kusarikku.name: Kusarikku
kusarikku.lore: The Kusarikku, or Bull-Man, is a Mesopotamian creature with the upper body of a man and the lower body of a bull. These hybrid beings served as divine guardians and were placed at temple gates to ward off evil. Some traditions place them as servants of Tiamat created for her cosmic war. Their horns carry the force of divine law.

beholder_spawn.name: Beholder Spawn
beholder_spawn.lore: A lesser offshoot of the legendary beholder, this aberrant orb of flesh drifts through the dungeon on faint telekinetic currents. Its central maw drips acid and its tiny eyestalks crackle with unfocused antimagic. Even diminished, its alien intellect makes it a terrifying opponent.

mind_flayer_thrall.name: Mind Flayer Thrall
mind_flayer_thrall.lore: Once a scholar or warrior, this humanoid has been reduced to a puppet by the psychic domination of an illithid master. Its blank eyes conceal a roiling telepathic assault. Those who survive its mind blast often wish they had not.

aboleth_lesser.name: Lesser Aboleth
aboleth_lesser.lore: An ancient piscine horror that remembers a world before gods. The lesser aboleth is nonetheless immensely powerful, projecting a mucus-covered field that transforms flesh to jelly. Its three eyes hold the accumulated malice of eons.

gibbering_mouther.name: Gibbering Mouther
gibbering_mouther.lore: A grotesque mass of mouths and eyes that flows across the dungeon floor. Its incoherent babbling is a psychic assault that reduces even the steadfast to babbling confusion. Each mouth drools a different fluid, none of them wholesome.

intellect_devourer.name: Intellect Devourer
intellect_devourer.lore: A walking brain on four clawed legs, this creature was bred by illithids as an assassin and scout. It seeks out sleeping prey and devours the mind whole, then animates the husk. The thought of it skittering through the dark keeps experienced dungeon delvers awake at night.

nothic.name: Nothic
nothic.lore: A wizard consumed by the desire for forbidden knowledge, warped by a curse into this one-eyed aberration. It giggles to itself and peers into shadows, gleaning secrets from those it watches. Its rotting gaze can reduce flesh to ruin in moments.

grell.name: Grell
grell.lore: A floating brain-like predator with a vicious beak and ten tentacles ending in paralytic barbs. The grell is utterly blind yet navigates with extraordinary precision through some unknown sensory faculty. It hunts by drifting silently near the ceiling and dropping on unsuspecting prey.

cloaker.name: Cloaker
cloaker.lore: Indistinguishable from a black cloak until it strikes, the cloaker is a manta-like creature that drapes itself over its victim to suffocate and blind. It emits a haunting moan that causes unease in all who hear it. More than one adventurer has reached for their cloak only to find it had teeth.

star_spawn.name: Star Spawn
star_spawn.lore: A herald of the Far Realm, the star spawn is a mass of writhing alien flesh that defies coherent description. Its passage through reality leaves psychic wounds in the fabric of sanity itself. Ancient tomes describe the star spawn as the footprints of something much larger and more terrible.

balor_lesser.name: Lesser Balor
balor_lesser.lore: A towering demon general wreathed in living flame. Even this diminished form of a balor stands twice the height of a man and radiates heat intense enough to scorch stone. When slain, it detonates in a catastrophic explosion of fiendish energy.

marilith_spawn.name: Marilith Spawn
marilith_spawn.lore: A six-armed serpentine demoness of lesser pedigree, still terrifying in martial skill. She wields multiple blades simultaneously with preternatural grace, and her coils can crush stone. Marilith spawn serve as battlefield commanders in the demon legions.

vrock.name: Vrock
vrock.lore: A vulture-headed demon of rage and destruction, the vrock shrills ear-splitting war cries and tears enemies apart with hooked talons. It periodically releases a cloud of toxic spores that root in living flesh and bloom grotesquely. The screech of a vrock flock is said to drive men mad.

hezrou.name: Hezrou
hezrou.lore: A massive toad-demon that reeks of an overpowering stench caustic enough to make eyes water and lungs seize. The hezrou hurls itself bodily into combat and smothers foes with its bulk. It is among the most common of the greater demons, serving as shock troops in demonic invasions.

glabrezu.name: Glabrezu
glabrezu.lore: A four-armed demon with massive crab-like pincers and a dog-like head, the glabrezu is a treacherous tempter who weaves corruption through whispered promises before tearing victims apart. Its great pincers can snap a man in two. It delights in ironic cruelty above all else.

nalfeshnee_lesser.name: Lesser Nalfeshnee
nalfeshnee_lesser.lore: A grotesquely obese demon with a boar-like snout who serves as a judge in the infernal hierarchy. Though it moves with deceptive sluggishness, its nimbus of chaotic energy is psychically devastating. It takes perverse pleasure in condemning souls to eternal suffering.

quasit.name: Quasit
quasit.lore: A tiny winged demon that serves as a familiar for warlocks and chaotic spellcasters. The quasit is spiteful and cunning far beyond its size, delighting in petty cruelties. When threatened it shapeshifts into a bat or centipede to flee — cowardice is its foremost virtue.

dretch.name: Dretch
dretch.lore: The lowliest of demons, wretched and barely coherent. Dretches exist in writhing masses on the lowest planes, each one a soul so corrupt it could not even achieve dignity in damnation. Their stench alone makes hardened soldiers retch.

shadow_demon.name: Shadow Demon
shadow_demon.lore: A demon reduced to pure shadow, either by ancient punishment or voluntary transformation. It phases through walls and extinguishes light sources as it passes. Only magical or silver weapons can reliably harm it, and it hungers for the warmth of living souls.

incubus.name: Incubus
incubus.lore: A demon of lust and corruption that assumes an unnervingly beautiful form to beguile victims. Its charm is not merely physical but psychically enforced — victims feel compelled to serve and protect it even as it drains the life from them. It collects trophies from those it has destroyed.

succubus_shade.name: Succubus Shade
succubus_shade.lore: A half-corporeal succubus whose physical form is laced with shadow, making her difficult to look at directly. She drifts through walls at will and feeds on the life force of the unwary sleeper. Those drained by her wake confused and weakened, unsure if they dreamed.

rutterkin.name: Rutterkin
rutterkin.lore: A wretched twisted demon just above the dretch in the infernal pecking order. Rutterkin are bullies who compensate for their low station with savage aggression toward anything weaker. They frequently travel in small packs, squabbling with each other even as they hunt.

mane_demon.name: Mane Demon
mane_demon.lore: The most basic demonic form — a soul so wicked it condenses into a mindless, clawing thing upon damnation. Manes exist only to serve and be destroyed. They attack in overwhelming numbers, their sheer volume compensating for individual feebleness.

chain_devil_spawn.name: Chain Devil Spawn
chain_devil_spawn.lore: A lesser kyton, festooned with animate chains that writhe of their own accord. The spawn lacks the full devilish cunning of its progenitors but compensates with raw aggression. It uses its chains to entangle prey before closing to deliver vicious blows.

bearded_devil.name: Bearded Devil
bearded_devil.lore: A front-line devil warrior with a matted fibrous beard that writhes with barbed filaments. Its wounds caused by the beard fester and resist healing. Bearded devils are disciplined soldiers who form phalanxes in the infernal armies.

barbed_devil.name: Barbed Devil
barbed_devil.lore: A hamatula encrusted in razor-sharp spines that tear at anything that comes near. Its burning aura makes proximity a choice between fighting it or catching fire. Barbed devils serve as guardians of infernal vaults and important prisoners.

bone_devil.name: Bone Devil
bone_devil.lore: An osyluth — a gaunt, scorpion-tailed devil of exceptional cunning. It serves as an overseer and enforcer in the hellish hierarchy, ferreting out weakness and disloyalty. The bone devil delights in long drawn-out torment, reserving its potent sting for those who have outlived their usefulness.

erinyes_lesser.name: Lesser Erinyes
erinyes_lesser.lore: A fallen angel twisted into devilhood, the lesser erinyes retains beautiful features that conceal absolute cruelty. It wields an enchanted longsword and a rope capable of binding even the mightiest heroes. Its tears, if shed at all, are for its own lost grace.

imp.name: Imp
imp.lore: A tiny red devil that serves as a warlock's familiar or a noble's secret advisor. The imp is clever, malicious, and utterly selfish — it serves only until a better opportunity presents itself. Its sting carries a potent diabolic venom.

lemure.name: Lemure
lemure.lore: The lowest form of devil — a damned soul reshaped into a blob of writhing infernal flesh. Lemures are mindless and nearly indestructible by mundane means; they regenerate rapidly and feel no pain. Only holy magic or fire can permanently destroy them.

pit_fiend_spawn.name: Pit Fiend Spawn
pit_fiend_spawn.lore: The generals of the Nine Hells, pit fiends are among the most powerful creatures that can be encountered in a dungeon — and even a spawn is only slightly less terrifying than the real thing. It commands legions, plots across centuries, and murders with the efficiency of a machine.

stone_golem_lesser.name: Lesser Stone Golem
stone_golem_lesser.lore: A masterwork of arcane engineering, the stone golem executes its orders with the remorseless weight of a small avalanche. Its slow aura radiates from the magic animating its core, causing all nearby to feel as if they move through treacle. Once a golem has a target, it does not tire or despair.

clay_golem.name: Clay Golem
clay_golem.lore: Animated clay shaped roughly into humanoid form, the clay golem has a berserk state that even its creator cannot always predict or control. When damaged it sometimes enters a murderous frenzy, striking wildly at everything around it. Its wounds heal rapidly in dry air.

wood_golem.name: Wood Golem
wood_golem.lore: A crude golem fashioned from carved timber and animated by a simple enchantment. The wood golem is stationary unless triggered, often used as a guardian for doors or chests. It burns readily and a torch can turn the tide against one rapidly.

animated_armor.name: Animated Armor
animated_armor.lore: A suit of plate armor animated by a poltergeist or magical command, the animated armor is a hollow warrior that protects an area long after its original master died. It is an eerie sight marching on its own, empty visor aimed at anything that moves.

shield_guardian.name: Shield Guardian
shield_guardian.lore: Built to protect a specific master, the shield guardian is a masterpiece of magical engineering. It can absorb spells cast at its ward and store them for later release. Without its master nearby it lapses into a simple guard routine, attacking all who approach.

homunculus.name: Homunculus
homunculus.lore: A tiny winged familiar crafted from blood and alchemical compounds by a skilled wizard. The homunculus shares a psychic link with its creator and relays sensory information back to them. Its bite carries a soporific toxin useful for subduing subjects without killing them.

clockwork_horror.name: Clockwork Horror
clockwork_horror.lore: A spider-like mechanical horror assembled from brass, copper, and unknown alloys. Clockwork horrors repair themselves and their kin, making them dangerous in groups. They were created as autonomous assassins by a long-dead artificer civilization and continue their ancient programming.

displacer_beast.name: Displacer Beast
displacer_beast.lore: A six-legged panther-like predator that projects a displaced image of itself, appearing to be slightly to the side of its actual location. This ability makes it infuriatingly difficult to strike while its two tentacles are lethally accurate. Displacer beasts hate blink dogs with an ancient and implacable passion.

owlbear.name: Owlbear
owlbear.lore: A miserable alchemical accident or experiment fused the savagery of a bear with the precision and ferocity of a great owl. The owlbear is perpetually hungry, furious, and difficult to reason with. Despite the chaos of its origin it breeds true, suggesting it is now a permanent fixture of the dungeon ecosystem.

black_pudding.name: Black Pudding
black_pudding.lore: The most dangerous of the common oozes, the black pudding dissolves metal and wood on contact. When struck with edged or lightning weapons it splits into smaller puddings, each just as deadly. Torches are the delver's best friend against this creature.

gray_ooze.name: Gray Ooze
gray_ooze.lore: A grey slick of animate protoplasm indistinguishable from wet stone in poor lighting. The gray ooze lurks motionless until prey blunders into it, then exudes a corrosive slime that degrades armor and metal equipment. It is immune to both fire and cold — the typical adventurer's solution to ooze problems.

ochre_jelly.name: Ochre Jelly
ochre_jelly.lore: An amber-yellow ooze that flows surprisingly quickly in pursuit of prey. Unlike the black pudding it cannot dissolve metal, but its acidic pseudopod delivers a burning corrosive that eats through leather and flesh. Lightning and blunt weapons cause it to split.

piercer.name: Piercer
piercer.lore: A mollusk-like creature that clings to cave ceilings disguised as a stalactite. When prey passes beneath it, the piercer drops with lethal accuracy, impaling the target on its pointed shell. After a miss it slowly crawls back up the wall to wait again — they are patient hunters.

lurker_above.name: Lurker Above
lurker_above.lore: Like the trapper but ceiling-dwelling, the lurker above disguises itself as a flat expanse of stone on the dungeon roof. It drops on prey and wraps around them, suffocating the life out. Many unwary adventurers have looked up, seen what appeared to be ordinary stone, and died for the error.

trapper.name: Trapper
trapper.lore: A flat creature that mimics dungeon flooring with terrifying perfection. When a creature walks across it the trapper folds up around the victim and begins squeezing and digesting. Veteran delvers tap the floor ahead of them with a pole — those who don't learn the habit often don't get the chance to.

roper.name: Roper
roper.lore: A stalagmite-like ambush predator with six sticky strands it can extend up to fifty feet. When a strand attaches it drains vitality from the victim while drawing them inexorably toward the roper's enormous toothy maw. They are disturbingly intelligent and seem to take pleasure in their prey's struggles.

carrion_crawler.name: Carrion Crawler
carrion_crawler.lore: An eight-tentacled worm the size of a horse that scavenges dungeon corridors for organic matter, living or dead. Its paralytic tentacles make it far more dangerous than it appears. It leaves trails of secretion on the stone that adventurers have learned to dread.

rot_grub_swarm.name: Rot Grub Swarm
rot_grub_swarm.lore: A writhing mass of pale finger-length worms found in refuse heaps and rotting carcasses. They burrow into flesh on contact and eat their way toward vital organs. Fire applied immediately to an entry wound is the standard — if brutal — treatment.

otyugh.name: Otyugh
otyugh.lore: A three-legged scavenger that lurks in refuse pits, sewers, and garbage heaps. Otyughs fill an ecological role as dungeon trash disposal, but are willing to consume anything organic that wanders within reach of their tentacles. Their bites cause festering infections that can kill long after the fight.

xorn.name: Xorn
xorn.lore: A three-armed, three-legged alien from the Elemental Plane of Earth. The xorn can phase through solid rock and has an exceptional sense for metals and gems, which it consumes for sustenance. It will negotiate for gold or gems and only fights when cornered or denied its prize.

gnoll_pack_lord.name: Gnoll Pack Lord
gnoll_pack_lord.lore: The alpha of a gnoll warband, scarred and massive from countless battles. A pack lord inspires the gnolls around it to greater ferocity — its death often breaks a warband's cohesion entirely. They collect the skulls of worthy enemies as trophies.

lizardfolk.name: Lizardfolk
lizardfolk.lore: Cold-blooded reptilian humanoids who view the world through a lens of brutal pragmatism. Lizardfolk eat their enemies, not out of malice but simple resource efficiency. They build warrens near water and defend their territory with coordinated ferocity.

lizardfolk_shaman.name: Lizardfolk Shaman
lizardfolk_shaman.lore: The spiritual leader of a lizardfolk tribe, the shaman communes with primal reptilian spirits to wield poison magic. They are revered and feared within their community, and killing one throws a tribe into disarray. Their totems are carved from the bones of their greatest enemies.

troglodyte.name: Troglodyte
troglodyte.lore: Primitive cave-dwelling lizard-men with an overpowering musk that causes nausea in those nearby. Troglodytes are savage and tribalistic, fighting over hunting grounds in the upper dungeon levels. Their society is built around strength, and the weakest are routinely eaten.

kobold_sorcerer.name: Kobold Sorcerer
kobold_sorcerer.lore: Among kobold-kind, those who manifest draconic sorcery are elevated to honored status. The kobold sorcerer channels the fire magic inherited from their distant dragon ancestry, hurling bolts from the shadows while their kin pepper enemies with crossbow bolts. A coward with real power is still dangerous.

bullywug.name: Bullywug
bullywug.lore: Aggressive frog-humanoids that infest swampy dungeon levels and underground waterways. Bullywugs leap great distances and attempt to overwhelm enemies with numbers. They serve more powerful creatures as shock troops in exchange for territory and scraps.

kuo_toa.name: Kuo-Toa
kuo_toa.lore: Fish-men of the deep dungeon who worship a mad pantheon of their own deranged creation. The kuo-toa have been driven partially insane by generations spent underground, worshiping gods they invented. Their shields are coated in a sticky secretion that catches weapons and holds them fast.

behir_lesser.name: Lesser Behir
behir_lesser.lore: A twelve-legged serpentine creature that crackles with stored electrical energy. The lesser behir lacks the full grown specimen's ability to swallow prey whole but its lightning breath and crushing coils are quite sufficient to kill most adventurers. It holds particular hatred for dragons.

catoblepas.name: Catoblepas
catoblepas.lore: A bloated buffalo-like creature whose neck is too weak to hold its enormous head upright — which is a mercy, since its gaze kills. When it manages to raise its head, anything caught in its eyes has a brief moment to regret their life choices. Its tail meanwhile delivers crushing blows.

cockatrice.name: Cockatrice
cockatrice.lore: A rooster-headed draconic creature whose beak petrifies on contact. The cockatrice is relatively fragile but its touch effect makes it extraordinarily dangerous — a single hit can immobilize a warrior in seconds. They are territorial and aggressive far beyond their actual threat level.

darkmantle.name: Darkmantle
darkmantle.lore: A tentacled cephalopod that hangs from cave ceilings, extinguishing light sources with a magical darkness it generates. It drops onto its prey's head and engulfs the face, blinding and suffocating simultaneously. Its darkness aura makes fighting it a disorienting experience.

ettercap.name: Ettercap
ettercap.lore: A spider-worshiping humanoid that builds elaborate web traps throughout its territory. The ettercap herds spiders as livestock and uses their silk in its own trapping. It is cunning enough to set ambushes and cowardly enough to watch prey exhaust themselves against its snares before closing.

grick.name: Grick
grick.lore: A tentacled worm that lurks in crevices and wall niches, blending perfectly with surrounding stone. Its hide deflects non-magical weapons, making it a nasty surprise for unprepared adventurers. When satisfied it retreats to its hole to digest; when hungry it is relentless.

hook_horror.name: Hook Horror
hook_horror.lore: A vulture-headed, beetle-bodied creature with powerful hooked forelimbs for climbing and striking. Hook horrors communicate via a series of clicks that echo through caverns, making their approach audible long before they arrive. Unfortunately this also means they travel in packs.

phase_spider.name: Phase Spider
phase_spider.lore: A giant spider with the ability to step between the material plane and the ethereal. It bites and phases out before retaliation is possible, making it one of the most frustrating creatures to fight. Its venom is extraordinarily potent and sought by assassins and alchemists alike.

wyvern.name: Wyvern
wyvern.lore: A dragon's lesser cousin — only two legs, no breath weapon, but a barbed tail with a stinger carrying lethal poison. The wyvern is less intelligent than a true dragon but compensates with raw aggression and physical power. Their stingers are prized components in antivenom and poison research.

skeleton_archer.name: Skeleton Archer
skeleton_archer.lore: The bones of fallen archers, animated by dark necromancy. They retain a grim instinct for ranged combat, loosing arrows with hollow eye sockets fixed on their prey. Their quivers are perpetually restocked by some unknown magical compulsion.

skeleton_warrior.name: Skeleton Warrior
skeleton_warrior.lore: Armored skeletons that once served as elite soldiers in life. Their martial training persists beyond death, making them more dangerous than common skeletons. The enchantments binding them to service were woven by powerful warlocks of a forgotten age.

skeleton_mage.name: Skeleton Mage
skeleton_mage.lore: The skeletal remains of wizards who refused to release their grip on magic even in death. Faint traces of arcane energy crackle between their finger bones as they hurl bolts of magical force. They are prized by necromancers for the residual spell knowledge trapped within their skulls.

burning_skeleton.name: Burning Skeleton
burning_skeleton.lore: Skeletons wreathed in unquenchable hellfire, condemned to burn for eternity without being consumed. The flames serve as both weapon and ward, scorching any who come too close. They are said to be the remains of those who trafficked with fire demons in life.

frost_skeleton.name: Frost Skeleton
frost_skeleton.lore: Skeletons encased in a permanent frost, their bones blue-white with cold energy. They were once warriors slain in frozen wastelands whose spirits merged with winter itself. A faint mist of freezing vapor trails their every movement.

zombie_hulk.name: Zombie Hulk
zombie_hulk.lore: Enormously swollen undead corpses, their muscles bloated by necromantic energy far beyond what they possessed in life. Though lumbering and slow, a single blow from their massive fists can send warriors flying. Necromancers create them as living battering rams.

plague_zombie.name: Plague Zombie
plague_zombie.lore: Rotting corpses reanimated during or after a devastating plague, their bodies still teeming with virulent disease. Even a scratch from their blackened fingernails can infect a healthy warrior with a debilitating illness. Wise adventurers deal with them from a distance whenever possible.

rotting_zombie.name: Rotting Zombie
rotting_zombie.lore: The most basic form of animated corpse, barely held together by decaying sinew and dark magic. They shamble forward with mindless persistence, threatening to overwhelm through sheer numbers. The stench alone is enough to make unprepared adventurers retch.

ghast.name: Ghast
ghast.lore: More powerful and cunning than common ghouls, ghasts retain a horrific parody of intelligence that they use to coordinate hunts and set ambushes. Their paralytic touch affects even those normally immune to such effects, and they exude a nauseating stench. They are known to collect trophies from particularly challenging prey.

gravehound.name: Gravehound
gravehound.lore: The skeletal remains of guard dogs and hunting hounds, reanimated to serve as tireless sentinels of tomb complexes. They hunt in packs, using the same cooperative tactics they employed in life to flank and bring down prey. Their bony jaws can snap shut with tremendous force.

poltergeist.name: Poltergeist
poltergeist.lore: Restless spirits that manifest as invisible forces hurling objects and causing mayhem. Unlike most undead they are not driven by hunger or hatred but by a confused anguish at their own death. The chaos they cause is an expression of frustration at being trapped between worlds.

revenant.name: Revenant
revenant.lore: The walking dead returned by pure force of will, driven by a singular obsession for vengeance against those who wronged them in life. A revenant will pursue its quarry across planes of existence until justice is satisfied. They are nearly impossible to permanently destroy until their vendetta is resolved.

allip.name: Allip
allip.lore: The insane remnant of a tormented soul, the allip is a whirling mass of incorporeal madness that babbles incoherently. Those who listen too carefully to its ravings risk being drawn into its lunacy. It hungers for the sanity it lost, attempting to drain wisdom from the living.

haunting_presence.name: Haunting Presence
haunting_presence.lore: A weak spirit bound to a specific location by trauma or unfinished business. It lacks the power to manifest physically but can project overwhelming dread onto those who enter its domain. Most mistake its cold presence for a mere chill wind until it is too late.

fetch.name: Fetch
fetch.lore: A doppelganger spirit that takes on the appearance of a living person's death omen. Seeing a fetch of yourself is said to portend imminent doom. They delight in mimicking the living before draining their life essence.

shade.name: Shade
shade.lore: A fragment of a departed soul that could not fully pass on, manifesting as a dim shadow without a source of light. Shades drift through darkened places seeking the warmth of life they can no longer possess. They are formed when souls are improperly laid to rest or violently separated from their bodies.

will_o_wisp.name: Will-o-Wisp
will_o_wisp.lore: Eerie glowing lights that drift through swamps and ruins, luring travelers to their doom with their hypnotic radiance. Will-o-wisps are the spirits of the malicious who delight even in death in leading the living astray. They feed on the fear and despair of those lost because of them.

soul_shadow.name: Soul Shadow
soul_shadow.lore: An amalgamation of multiple departed souls fused into a single shadowy entity by catastrophic violence or powerful dark magic. The souls within are in constant anguish, driving the composite creature to spread misery. It drains the vital essence of living beings, adding fragments of their souls to its own mass.

death_echo.name: Death Echo
death_echo.lore: A psychic impression of a violent death, replaying the moment of its creation endlessly. The death echo appears as a translucent reenactment of its own demise, lashing out at anything that disturbs its loop. Scholars believe these manifestations form when death is so traumatic it leaves a permanent scar on reality.

vampire_thrall.name: Vampire Thrall
vampire_thrall.lore: Living servants of vampires who have been fed small quantities of vampire blood, granting them supernatural strength and unbreakable loyalty. They have not yet died and risen again, existing in a halfway state between life and undeath. They will sacrifice themselves without hesitation to protect their master.

nosferatu.name: Nosferatu
nosferatu.lore: Ancient vampires of a degenerate bloodline, more monstrous than noble in appearance. Unlike their aristocratic cousins, nosferatu embrace the bestial nature of vampirism, becoming physically repulsive creatures of tremendous power. They are solitary hunters who have had centuries to perfect their predatory craft.

strigoi.name: Strigoi
strigoi.lore: A vampire variant from eastern tradition said to be the result of the truly wicked dying without proper burial rites. The strigoi can take animal forms and spreads disease through its corrupted blood. Village folk tell of strigoi returning to drain the life from family members first before seeking other prey.

jiangshi.name: Jiangshi
jiangshi.lore: A hopping corpse from eastern tradition, preserved by magical paper talismans and animated by residual spiritual energy. They absorb the chi of living beings through touch, leaving victims completely drained. During the day they are rigid and helpless, but at night they hunt with terrifying determination.

vrykolakas.name: Vrykolakas
vrykolakas.lore: A Greek vampire of immense physical power, bloated with stolen blood and nearly indestructible without specific ritualistic destruction. The vrykolakas was typically an excommunicated individual or one who died in a state of sin. They return to trouble the living with plague, pestilence, and direct violence.

demi_lich.name: Demi-Lich
demi_lich.lore: A lich so ancient that its body has crumbled away, leaving only the skull which houses its immortal consciousness. The demi-lich has transcended physical existence almost entirely, its soul focused into the gems embedded in its eye sockets. Its very wail can trap the souls of the living, and it commands magic beyond mortal comprehension.

lich_thrall.name: Lich Thrall
lich_thrall.lore: Powerful undead spellcasters created by liches to serve as lieutenants and guardians. Unlike common undead, lich thralls retain enough intelligence to cast spells and follow complex orders. They are bound by magical oaths of loyalty that persist beyond any physical compulsion.

death_mage.name: Death Mage
death_mage.lore: Mortal wizards who voluntarily underwent partial undead transformation to extend their studies of necromancy. They have not yet committed to full lichdom, existing in a twilight state between life and undeath. Their command of death magic is exceptional, though their partially deceased state makes them emotionally unstable.

bone_witch.name: Bone Witch
bone_witch.lore: Ancient witches who chose undeath over mortality, their bodies preserved by dark ritual into a skeletal state while retaining their cunning minds. They collect and animate the bones of fallen enemies, using them as both weapons and decorations in their lairs. Bone witches are known to hoard knowledge of forbidden spells.

necromancer_lord.name: Necromancer Lord
necromancer_lord.lore: A master of necromantic arts who has achieved partial undeath, becoming something between a living mage and a lich. They command armies of undead with casual ease, animating fallen enemies on the battlefield to replace fallen minions. Their phylacteries are rumored to be hidden within other undead creatures under their command.

pale_master.name: Pale Master
pale_master.lore: Necromancers who eschewed the traditional path to lichdom in favor of grafting undead flesh and bone directly to their living bodies. Over time, the boundary between their living and undead components blurs until they are something entirely unique. Their intimate knowledge of undead physiology makes their magic particularly effective.

wight_lord.name: Wight Lord
wight_lord.lore: A wight who has accumulated centuries of strength and taken dominion over lesser undead in a territory. Wight lords retain more of their original personality than common wights, using this cunning to organize their undead underlings into effective raiding parties. Their life-draining touch is especially potent, having been honed across hundreds of victims.

barrow_wight.name: Barrow Wight
barrow_wight.lore: Ancient kings and chieftains entombed with their treasures in burial mounds, cursed to guard their hoards for eternity. Barrow wights resent the living for the freedom they no longer possess, attacking intruders with cold fury. They are bound to their barrow and suffer increasing agony the further they stray from it.

cairn_wight.name: Cairn Wight
cairn_wight.lore: Wights bound to simple stone cairn burial markers rather than elaborate tomb complexes. They are weaker than their barrow-dwelling kin but no less dangerous to unprepared travelers. Cairn wights often mark the graves of warriors who fell in battle far from home.

crypt_thing.name: Crypt Thing
crypt_thing.lore: A unique form of undead guardian created specifically to protect tombs without causing harm to the protected dead within. Rather than attacking intruders directly, crypt things teleport trespassers to random locations in the dungeon, separating adventuring parties and leaving individuals lost and alone. They cannot be reasoned with or bribed.

grave_guardian.name: Grave Guardian
grave_guardian.lore: Powerful undead warriors voluntarily entombed as eternal guardians of sacred burial sites. They were once legendary champions who swore oaths to protect the honored dead. Curiously, they wield holy power that is effective against other undead, though they themselves are undead — a testament to the complexity of their sacred duty.

tomb_guardian.name: Tomb Guardian
tomb_guardian.lore: Elite undead sentinels created through a combination of necromancy and binding magic to guard the most important royal tombs. Each tomb guardian was specifically crafted for a single purpose and a single location, becoming more powerful over the centuries as the magic deepens. Tomb guardians become increasingly desperate and violent the deeper into their charge's tomb an intruder penetrates.

ossuary_horror.name: Ossuary Horror
ossuary_horror.lore: A vast shifting mass of thousands of bones animated as a single collective entity. When an ossuary is disturbed by dark magic, the combined spiritual residue of all interred bones can coalesce into this abomination. It can extend pseudopods of bone in any direction and absorb the skeletons of defeated enemies into its own bulk.

mohrg.name: Mohrg
mohrg.lore: The animated corpses of mass murderers and serial killers, driven by the same murderous impulses that defined them in life. A mohrg's most terrifying weapon is its visceral tongue, which can paralyze victims with a touch. They compulsively create spawn from those they slay, seeking to build a following of undead murderers.

huecuva.name: Huecuva
huecuva.lore: The undead remains of clerics and holy men who abandoned their faith, condemned to walk as undead mockeries of their former devoted selves. They often appear as illusions of living priests to deceive travelers seeking aid. Their touch spreads a disease that specifically corrupts divine magic, making them particularly dangerous to clerics.

lesser_death.name: Lesser Death
lesser_death.lore: A minor manifestation of Death itself, neither truly undead nor truly alive but an avatar of ending. They are drawn to places where death is common, feeding on the transition of souls from life to whatever lies beyond. Scholars debate whether lesser deaths are actually undead or simply personifications of a cosmic force wearing an undead guise.

greater_shadow.name: Greater Shadow
greater_shadow.lore: An ancient and powerful shadow that has fed on living vitality for so long it has grown to monstrous proportions. It can swallow entire rooms in supernatural darkness and devour the strength of all within. Creatures slain by a greater shadow sometimes rise as lesser shadows almost immediately.

nightshade_spawn.name: Nightshade Spawn
nightshade_spawn.lore: Offspring of true nightshade creatures, these lesser versions of the nightshade family are still among the most powerful undead a dungeon delver is likely to encounter. They embody the concept of negative energy given physical form, corrupting and desecrating everything around them. Plants wither, fire dims, and hope fades in their presence.
