forsaken_shell.name: Forsaken Shell
forsaken_shell.lore: An animated body from which the soul has been violently ripped, leaving a hollow vessel that moves on pure necromantic energy alone. The forsaken shell retains no memories or personality of its former occupant. They are sometimes created deliberately by necromancers who need the soul for other purposes but want to keep the body useful.

hollow_one.name: Hollow One
hollow_one.lore: A person who traded away their soul through dark bargains, died, and now walks as an undead creature of perpetual hunger. The hollow one seeks souls to fill the void where their own used to be, but no soul can permanently fill the absence. They are pitiable creatures, driven mad by an emptiness that cannot be sated.

charnel_hound.name: Charnel Hound
charnel_hound.lore: Large skeletal dogs assembled from the bones of multiple animals, animated to serve as fast-moving hunters and trackers. Charnel hounds can follow a scent trail through the ethereal plane, tracking prey across impossible distances. They are frequently employed by necromancers as scouts and to run down fleeing intruders.

dread_knight.name: Dread Knight
dread_knight.lore: Fallen paladins who have become undead warriors of darkness, their divine power twisted into unholy strength. They retain their martial prowess and tactical intelligence from life, making them among the most dangerous undead combatants. Their armor seems to absorb light, and their gaze carries the weight of condemned souls.

bone_archer.name: Bone Archer
bone_archer.lore: Skeletal archers animated with the sole purpose of providing undead ranged fire support. Their arrows are carved from the same bones that compose their fingers, magically regenerating after each shot. Bone archers prefer elevated positions and will attempt to maintain distance from melee opponents.

skeletal_giant.name: Skeletal Giant
skeletal_giant.lore: The reanimated skeleton of a giant, towering over ordinary undead at three to four times human height. A single blow from its massive fists can flatten an armored knight. Skeletal giants are so powerful that most necromancers who create them quickly lose control of the results.

undead_dragon_lesser.name: Lesser Undead Dragon
undead_dragon_lesser.lore: A draconic corpse reanimated through necromantic art of staggering complexity. Lesser undead dragons retain fragments of their former cunning, making them far more dangerous than other undead of similar power. Their breath weapon now carries the chill of the grave rather than elemental fire or frost.

lich_dragon_spawn.name: Lich Dragon Spawn
lich_dragon_spawn.lore: The pinnacle of necromantic achievement: a dragon transformed into a draconic lich. This creature possesses the full cunning of a dragon, the immortality of a lich, and the combined magical power of both. Lich dragon spawn are created when a true lich convinces or coerces a dragon into the phylactery ritual. They are among the most feared creatures in existence.

ethereal_filcher.name: Ethereal Filcher
ethereal_filcher.lore: An incorporeal undead that phases between the material and ethereal planes to steal objects from the living. Ethereal filchers are drawn to bright or magical items, which they collect in hidden ethereal lairs. They are rarely aggressive unless cornered, preferring to flee with their stolen goods rather than risk destruction.

nightmare_shade.name: Nightmare Shade
nightmare_shade.lore: Undead born from the accumulated nightmares of those who died in absolute terror. A nightmare shade embodies the worst fears its progenitor experienced and projects them outward to paralyze victims with dread. They can change shape to become the specific fear of whoever they are facing.

soul_eater_spawn.name: Soul Eater Spawn
soul_eater_spawn.lore: Offspring of soul eaters, ravenous undead spirits that exist solely to consume the spiritual essence of living beings. Each soul they consume adds to their own power, theoretically allowing them to grow without limit. Soul eater spawn are distinguishable from their parents only by their slightly smaller size and reduced power.

dread_wraith.name: Dread Wraith
dread_wraith.lore: Ancient wraiths of tremendous power, dread wraiths are what common wraiths aspire to become after centuries of draining life from the living. They are rarely seen as they prefer to operate from the ethereal plane, reaching through to drain victims without exposing themselves to retaliation. The area around a dread wraith is perpetually cold and lightless.

nightwing.name: Nightwing
nightwing.lore: The greatest of the nightshade creatures, a nightwing appears as a vast bat-like shadow of impossible size that blots out stars as it passes overhead. They are engines of pure negative energy that desecrate the land they fly over, killing plant life and corrupting water sources. Ancient texts warn that a nightwing's arrival heralds a period of mass death and catastrophe.

crystal_ooze.name: crystal ooze
crystal_ooze.lore: A translucent, slow-moving ooze whose body refracts light into prismatic shards. Its crystalline pseudopods can slow victims with a coating of hardening mineral film. Miners fear these creatures for their habit of lurking in gem veins.

green_slime.name: green slime
green_slime.lore: Green slime clings to dungeon ceilings and drips onto unsuspecting prey. Contact with its acidic body quickly dissolves organic matter. Alchemists prize small samples for use in corrosive reagents.

yellow_mold.name: yellow mold
yellow_mold.lore: This bright fungal growth ejects clouds of hallucinogenic spores when disturbed. Adventurers struck by the cloud often wander aimlessly, laughing at invisible dangers. The spores are highly flammable and burn with a vivid orange flame.

brown_mold.name: brown mold
brown_mold.lore: Brown mold paradoxically absorbs heat from the surrounding air, chilling any creature that draws near. Fire used against it only makes it spread faster and grow colder. The crust it leaves on dungeon walls can be scraped off for use in cooling potions.

gibbering_slime.name: gibbering slime
gibbering_slime.lore: A pulsating mass that constantly emits maddening nonsense syllables from dozens of half-formed mouths. Prolonged exposure to its gibbering induces confusion and terror. Scholars debate whether it possesses some fractured form of intelligence.

toxic_jelly.name: toxic jelly
toxic_jelly.lore: A vibrant green jelly that carries a virulent toxin in every cell of its body. Any wound it inflicts quickly turns septic without magical treatment. Herbalists use the diluted jelly as a powerful antiseptic in small doses.

giant_scorpion.name: giant scorpion
giant_scorpion.lore: This horse-sized arachnid is among the deadliest creatures in the dungeon's middle depths. Its pincers can crush bones outright, while its stinger delivers a paralytic venom. Desert tribes craft armor from its chitinous shell.

giant_wasp.name: giant wasp
giant_wasp.lore: Giant wasps hunt in buzzing clouds and are fiercely territorial around their paper nests. A single sting delivers enough venom to fell a grown man in minutes. Their larval chambers sometimes contain the preserved bodies of paralyzed prey.

giant_beetle.name: giant beetle
giant_beetle.lore: Armored in a thick iridescent carapace, the giant beetle is a surprisingly durable foe. Its mandibles exert crushing force capable of biting through chain mail. Armorers prize the polished shell plates as decorative reinforcements.

giant_mantis.name: giant mantis
giant_mantis.lore: The giant mantis is a patient ambush predator that can remain perfectly motionless for hours. When it strikes, it moves with terrifying speed, its serrated forelegs capable of decapitating a man. Its compound eyes give it a nearly 360-degree field of vision.

spider_queen.name: spider queen
spider_queen.lore: The matriarch of spider colonies, the spider queen is an immense eight-legged nightmare covered in dense black hair. She communicates with lesser spiders through pheromones and can direct dozens of them simultaneously. Her throne room is an elaborate web-cathedral hung with cocooned prey.

phase_spider_lesser.name: phase spider lesser
phase_spider_lesser.lore: A smaller cousin of the true phase spider, capable of briefly stepping between planes to avoid attacks. It blinks in and out of visibility during combat, making it extremely difficult to strike. Its silk retains a faint magical shimmer even after the spider's death.

web_spinner.name: web spinner
web_spinner.lore: Web spinners are small but prolific spiders that festoon dungeon corridors with sticky traps. They prefer to let their webs do the fighting, retreating when prey proves too dangerous. Their silk is prized by tailors for its remarkable tensile strength.

death_beetle.name: death beetle
death_beetle.lore: The death beetle is a massive, obsidian-shelled horror associated with necromantic energies. Its carapace seems to absorb light itself, and its presence is heralded by an eerie rattling sound. Ancient cultures carved its likeness onto tomb doors to ward off grave robbers.

giant_lizard.name: giant lizard
giant_lizard.lore: Giant lizards are opportunistic predators that inhabit warm dungeon levels and cave systems. They rely on ambush tactics, remaining motionless until prey is within striking distance. Their meat is considered a delicacy in some underground settlements.

basilisk_lesser.name: lesser basilisk
basilisk_lesser.lore: A juvenile basilisk whose petrifying gaze is not yet fully developed, causing paralysis rather than true petrification. It is nonetheless an extremely dangerous predator whose rocky lair is usually littered with frozen prey. Herbalists seek its eyes for polymorph-related brews.

rock_python.name: rock python
rock_python.lore: Rock pythons are tremendous constrictors that lurk among boulders and tunnel crevices. They can swallow prey the size of a dwarf whole, though they prefer to crush victims first. Their thick, patterned scales are used to make exceptionally supple leather goods.

komodo_brute.name: komodo brute
komodo_brute.lore: A brutish giant lizard whose mouth teems with vile bacteria, making every bite a slow death sentence. It tracks wounded prey for miles, patiently waiting for infection to do its work. The jawbone of a komodo brute is sometimes used as a morbid trophy.

giant_frog.name: giant frog
giant_frog.lore: Giant frogs lurk in subterranean pools and wet tunnels, leaping upon prey with shocking speed. Their sticky tongues can snatch a torch from a hand at ten feet. In desperate times, their legs provide a passable meal.

mud_crawler.name: mud crawler
mud_crawler.lore: Mud crawlers are squat, heavily armored reptiles that hug subterranean waterways. Their thick mud-caked hide is nearly impervious to slashing weapons. They move slowly but are almost impossible to dislodge once they have secured a grip.

swamp_serpent.name: swamp serpent
swamp_serpent.lore: The swamp serpent is an aggressive aquatic snake that lurks in dungeon flooded chambers and underground rivers. Its potent hemotoxic venom causes rapid hemorrhaging in its victims. Local cultures use its shed skin as waterproofing material for boats and bags.

sea_snake.name: sea snake
sea_snake.lore: Sea snakes possess among the most potent neurotoxic venoms of any serpent, causing rapid paralysis. They are aggressive when cornered and capable of swimming faster than most men can run. Divers near coastal ruins have learned to fear these brightly banded hunters.

dire_lion.name: dire lion
dire_lion.lore: The dire lion is a magnificent and terrifying predator nearly twice the size of its mundane cousin. Its roar is said to shake loose stalactites from cave ceilings. Prides led by a dire lion claim enormous territories and drive off all competition.

dire_bear.name: dire bear
dire_bear.lore: Dire bears are primordial ursines the size of draft horses, possessed of immeasurable strength. They are normally reclusive but defend their territories with catastrophic violence. Their thick pelts are worth a fortune to the right furrier.

wolf_pack_leader.name: wolf pack leader
wolf_pack_leader.lore: Pack leaders are massive wolves that coordinate their brethren with complex howls and body language. They direct flanking maneuvers and coordinated takedowns with uncanny intelligence. Killing a pack leader often causes the rest of the pack to scatter.

werewolf.name: werewolf
werewolf.lore: A cursed human transformed by the moon's influence into a savage wolf-man hybrid. In hybrid form the werewolf possesses the cunning of a person and the ferocity of a beast. Its blood carries the lycanthropic curse, transmissible through bites.

werewolf_alpha.name: werewolf alpha
werewolf_alpha.lore: A centuries-old lycanthrope who has mastered the curse, maintaining control even under the full moon. The alpha can transform at will and commands every wolf and werewolf in its territory. Its howl has been known to drive even veteran warriors mad with fear.

werebear.name: werebear
werebear.lore: Werebears are among the more tragic lycanthropes, often struggling against their feral nature. In hybrid form they become immovable juggernauts capable of ripping iron doors from hinges. Unlike werewolves they do not seek victims, but their rage makes them no less deadly.

wererat.name: wererat
wererat.lore: Wererats skulk through sewer systems and dungeon middens, often working as thieves and informants. They prefer to avoid direct combat, using hit-and-run tactics and their knowledge of hidden passages. Their bite carries a diseased form of the lycanthropic curse.

weretiger.name: weretiger
weretiger.lore: The weretiger is the apex predator among lycanthropes, combining human cunning with the hunting mastery of a tiger. It is known to stalk prey for days, learning its patterns before striking with terrifying precision. Ancient eastern traditions revere the weretiger as a divine judge of the unworthy.

winter_wolf.name: winter wolf
winter_wolf.lore: Winter wolves are intelligent arctic beasts who can speak in a guttural language and breathe blasts of killing frost. They sometimes serve frost giants as hunters and trackers. Their beautiful white pelts are worth a king's ransom to wealthy furriers.

blink_dog.name: blink dog
blink_dog.lore: Blink dogs are intelligent teleporting canines that live in packs and are considered a good omen by travelers. They teleport short distances to attack and immediately blink away, making them extremely hard to pin down. Despite their chaotic fighting style they are deeply loyal creatures who can be befriended.

death_dog.name: death dog
death_dog.lore: Death dogs are two-headed fiendish hounds whose bite delivers a supernatural rotting disease. They are often found serving undead lords or guarding cursed tombs. The fangs of a death dog are prized components in necromantic rituals.

giant_eagle.name: giant eagle
giant_eagle.lore: Giant eagles are proud and intelligent raptors that nest on the highest dungeon peaks and mountain crags. They are sometimes willing to parley with respectful adventurers and occasionally serve as aerial mounts. A single tail feather is worth more than most peasants earn in a year.

roc_chick.name: roc chick
roc_chick.lore: Even a roc chick is a formidable beast, standing taller than a horse and possessing a voracious appetite. The parents defend nesting grounds with world-shaking ferocity. An unhatched roc egg is worth enough gold to buy a small kingdom.

roc_adult.name: roc adult
roc_adult.lore: A fully grown roc blots out the sun and creates windstorms with a single wingbeat. Ancient rocs have been known to carry off elephants as snacks for their young. Legends say that every roc feather contains a wish, if one knows the right ritual to release it.

stirge_swarm.name: stirge swarm
stirge_swarm.lore: Stirges are bloodsucking mosquito-like creatures that travel in dense swarms, attaching to exposed flesh with barbed proboscises. A large enough swarm can drain a horse dry in minutes. Their wings make a high-pitched whine that alerts dungeon veterans to take cover.

tengu_warrior.name: tengu warrior
tengu_warrior.lore: Tengu warriors are crow-headed humanoid creatures who are master swordsmen of legendary skill. They occupy high dungeon places near wind channels and are fiercely territorial. Some rare tengu serve as sword-instructors to mortals who prove themselves worthy.

thunderbird_lesser.name: lesser thunderbird
thunderbird_lesser.lore: The lesser thunderbird is a magnificent storm-hawk that generates electrical fields across its plumage. Each wingbeat crackles with arcs of lightning and its cries shake stone walls. Its feathers remain charged for months after the bird's death, making them prized magical components.

pixie_swarm.name: pixie swarm
pixie_swarm.lore: Individually harmless, a swarm of pixies is a nuisance capable of putting entire parties to sleep with enchanted dust. They delight in mischief and have an instinctive hostility toward heavily armed visitors. Pixie dust collected after they depart retains potent soporific properties.

redcap.name: redcap
redcap.lore: Redcaps are murderous fey that dye their caps in the blood of their victims and must kill regularly or fade away. They are supernaturally strong for their diminutive size, favoring heavy iron boots and long pikes. Their iron-shod feet can crush stone, and their laughter is deeply unsettling.

will_o_wisp_lesser.name: will-o'-wisp (lesser)
will_o_wisp_lesser.lore: Lesser will-o'-wisps are ghostly lights that lure travelers into bogs and pits by mimicking torch light. They feed on the fear and despair of those they lead to doom. They are notoriously difficult to harm, as most weapons simply pass through their luminous forms.

green_hag.name: green hag
green_hag.lore: Green hags dwell in fetid swamps and use powerful illusions to appear beautiful until they are close enough to strike. They subsist on misery, feeding emotionally on despair they deliberately cause. Covens of three hags can perform terrifying magical rituals far beyond their individual capabilities.

sea_hag.name: sea hag
sea_hag.lore: Sea hags are hideous aquatic fey whose faces are so terrible that a prolonged gaze can strike a creature dead of fright. They lurk in coastal caves and underwater dungeons, collecting trinkets from drowned sailors. Their appearance is so awful that mirrors break in their presence.

annis_hag.name: annis hag
annis_hag.lore: Annis hags are massive, iron-skinned fey who prefer brute force over trickery. A single annis can overpower a giant in a wrestling match, and their claws can shred metal armor. They wear cloaks made from the skins of their victims and smell of blood and iron.

night_hag.name: night hag
night_hag.lore: Night hags are among the most feared beings in existence, traveling the ethereal plane to torment sleeping mortals. They collect the souls of those they drive to evil in soul-trapping gems called heartstones. Any witch seeking to ascend must first broker a deal with a night hag.

satyr_trickster.name: satyr trickster
satyr_trickster.lore: Satyr tricksters are hedonistic goat-men who use enchanted music to disorient and rob travelers. They rarely seek to kill, preferring to steal valuables and vanish into the forest laughing. Their panpipes are occasionally left behind as powerful magical instruments.

dryad_guardian.name: dryad guardian
dryad_guardian.lore: A dryad guardian is an arboreal fey who has chosen to actively defend her grove rather than hide within her tree. She can step between trees at will and commands the vegetation of her domain as extensions of her own body. Those who threaten her forest find roots rising to bind their feet.

leprechaun_hostile.name: hostile leprechaun
leprechaun_hostile.lore: Not all leprechauns are willing to grant wishes — some who have been robbed too many times have become openly hostile to adventurers. They use powerful illusions and misdirection before lashing out with surprising ferocity. Despite their size their pots of gold are very real.

spriggan.name: spriggan
spriggan.lore: Spriggans are grotesque fairy guards who can expand to enormous size to protect their masters' treasures. In their enlarged form they possess tremendous strength but lose much of their natural agility. They are notorious thieves who hoard the stolen goods of travelers.

boggart.name: boggart
boggart.lore: Boggarts are mischievous household fey that have turned malevolent, taking delight in spoiling food, pinching sleepers, and causing minor disasters. They rarely fight directly, preferring to haunt and confuse before fleeing into shadows. A boggart attached to a dungeon room can make that room nearly uninhabitable.

water_elemental.name: water elemental
water_elemental.lore: Water elementals rise from deep underground lakes and flooded dungeon chambers, taking the form of towering churning waves. They drag victims beneath the surface and hold them until they stop struggling. The core of a water elemental remains perpetually cold and is sought after by cryomancers.

air_elemental.name: air elemental
air_elemental.lore: Air elementals are near-invisible whirlwinds that can move at incredible speed through dungeon corridors. Their invisible slashing winds can strip flesh from bone in moments. The compressed air core at their center is prized by enchanters for use in flight-related magic items.

ice_elemental.name: ice elemental
ice_elemental.lore: Ice elementals are crystalline giants born from the deepest glacial caverns and the most savage arctic storms. Their touch coats victims in a sheath of magical ice that slows their movements. The ice core at their heart never melts and remains supernaturally cold even in volcanic environments.

magma_elemental.name: magma elemental
magma_elemental.lore: Magma elementals rise from volcanic vents in the deep dungeon, their ponderous bodies of living rock suffused with molten fire. Everything they touch ignites, and their footprints remain glowing for hours. A magma elemental core is among the most coveted items in pyromantic alchemy.

void_elemental.name: void elemental
void_elemental.lore: Void elementals originate from the space between planes, manifesting as patches of absolute darkness that devour light and sound. Their touch unravels the bonds of matter, leaving victims partially phased out of existence. A void shard from their core is one of the most dangerous substances in existence.

shambling_mound.name: shambling mound
shambling_mound.lore: Shambling mounds are massive ambulatory heaps of rotting vegetation animated by some swampy life-force or lightning strike. They crush and engulf prey, slowly digesting organic matter within their bulk. Lightning actually heals and invigorates them rather than causing damage.

assassin_vine.name: assassin vine
assassin_vine.lore: Assassin vines lurk motionless in dungeon gardens and along corridors, indistinguishable from ordinary vegetation. When prey passes within reach, tendrils shoot out to grab and constrict with crushing force. Their sap contains a mild paralytic useful in herbalist preparations.

thorn_slinger.name: thorn slinger
thorn_slinger.lore: Thorn slingers are sedentary plants that expel barrages of barbed, poison-coated thorns when any creature enters their range. They position themselves at chokepoints and room entrances for maximum effect. The thorns retain their poison for weeks after being expelled from the plant.

myconid_worker.name: myconid worker
myconid_worker.lore: Myconid workers are ambulatory mushroom folk who tend the vast underground gardens of their colonies. They are peaceful by nature but will defend their circles by releasing clouds of hallucinogenic spores. Their spores are a primary component in potions of confusion and certain dream remedies.

myconid_sovereign.name: myconid sovereign
myconid_sovereign.lore: Myconid sovereigns are ancient, enormous mushroom kings who govern entire subterranean fungal kingdoms through psychic spore networks. Their mind-altering spores can suppress the will of entire adventuring parties. The cap of a slain sovereign grows back as a fully sentient mushroom forest.

yellow_musk_creeper.name: yellow musk creeper
yellow_musk_creeper.lore: The yellow musk creeper produces intoxicating clouds of yellow pollen that overwhelm the minds of victims, drawing them into range of its grasping tendrils. Mindless thralls under its influence protect it and carry decomposing organic matter to fertilize its roots. The musk pollen alone is enough to drive most animals into docile confusion.

gas_spore.name: gas spore
gas_spore.lore: Gas spores are floating fungal pods that superficially resemble beholders, leading to fatal cases of mistaken identity among dungeon veterans. Any puncture causes them to detonate in a cloud of toxic spores. Those slain by the gas spore become hosts for new pods within hours.

violet_fungus.name: violet fungus
violet_fungus.lore: Violet fungi lurk in the dark, extending their tendrils to deliver a rotting touch that causes flesh to rapidly decay. They are often found clustered near gray ooze, with which they share a symbiotic relationship. In small quantities their extract is used in controlled decomposition experiments by alchemists.

pegasus_corrupted.name: corrupted pegasus
pegasus_corrupted.lore: Once a noble steed of the gods, this pegasus was twisted by shadow magic into a creature of darkness. Its formerly white flanks are now mottled black and its eyes glow a sickly purple. A courageous wizard who breaks the curse might restore it — but few survive the attempt.

unicorn_corrupted.name: corrupted unicorn
unicorn_corrupted.lore: A unicorn whose pure heart was corrupted by prolonged exposure to shadow energies, its once-golden horn now a jagged black spiral that drips with shadow-venom. It rages through dungeons destroying anything that once reminded it of its former life. The corrupted horn fragment retains terrible magical power.

sphinx_lesser.name: lesser sphinx
sphinx_lesser.lore: Lesser sphinxes guard ancient vaults and archives, compelling all who approach to answer riddles on pain of death. Unlike the great sphinxes, they lack godlike patience and grow violent when bored. Their accumulated knowledge of arcane lore makes defeating one feel like losing an irreplaceable library.

nue.name: nue
nue.lore: The nue is a terrifying chimeric creature from eastern legend with the head of a monkey, the body of a tanuki, the legs of a tiger, and the tail of a serpent. It brings misfortune and illness, and its shriek is said to induce nightmares in all who hear it. A quill from its serpentine tail is a powerful talisman against illness.

peryton.name: peryton
peryton.lore: The peryton is a winged stag of terrible omen whose shadow takes the shape of a human rather than its own form. It hunts intelligent prey specifically, drawn by some dark need to harvest humanoid hearts. The antlers of a peryton are said to grant prophetic visions when carved into a drinking cup.

carbuncle.name: carbuncle
carbuncle.lore: The carbuncle is a small, fox-like magical creature with a brilliant gemstone embedded in its forehead. It uses the gem to flash blinding light at predators before fleeing at tremendous speed. The gem, if carefully extracted after death, is among the most powerful magical focal points that can be found in nature.

sea_dragon_spawn.name: sea dragon spawn
sea_dragon_spawn.lore: Sea dragon spawn are juvenile dragons of the deep ocean, capable of pressurizing water into devastating focused jets. Even as young dragons they dwarf most surface creatures and possess considerable cunning. Their scales are among the most prized armor materials in any coastal nation.

goblin_shaman.name: goblin shaman
goblin_shaman.lore: Goblin shamans wield crude but effective tribal magic, channeling the malevolent will of their dark patron into hexes and curses. They hang back in combat, directing their kin with screeching commands. Their ritual masks are carved from dungeon mushroom caps soaked in alchemical reagents.

goblin_king.name: goblin king
goblin_king.lore: A goblin king rules through a combination of violence, cunning, and sheer volume. They are always larger than their subjects — sometimes naturally, sometimes through magical augmentation from their shaman advisors. Goblin kings wear crowns assembled from bent copper coins and teeth taken from rivals.

hobgoblin_captain.name: hobgoblin captain
hobgoblin_captain.lore: Hobgoblin captains have earned their rank through battlefield promotions in brutal campaigns. They carry unit banners that inspire nearby hobgoblins to fight with greater discipline. Their tactical acumen is impressive — they will always attempt to flank and cut off retreat before engaging.

hobgoblin_warlord.name: hobgoblin warlord
hobgoblin_warlord.lore: A hobgoblin warlord has survived dozens of battles through superior strategy and personal ferocity. They command absolute loyalty from their troops through a code of martial honor that punishes cowardice with death. The war trophies on their armor represent the most dangerous opponents they have personally defeated.

bugbear.name: bugbear
bugbear.lore: Bugbears are the largest of the common goblinoids, standing seven feet of muscle and fur. They are ambush predators who delight in causing fear before striking. Their natural stealth is uncanny for creatures their size — the silence before a bugbear attack is its only warning.

bugbear_chieftain.name: bugbear chieftain
bugbear_chieftain.lore: A bugbear chieftain has defeated every other bugbear in its territory. They rule with casual brutality, expecting tribute from lesser monsters in their vicinity. Their war trophies include the skulls of previous chieftains — a grim decoration that communicates their dominance without words.

orc_berserker.name: orc berserker
orc_berserker.lore: Orc berserkers enter a blood rage in combat, attacking with total disregard for their own safety. They feel no pain while berserking — wounds that would drop a normal warrior only fuel their fury. Their warband sends them in first as a shock unit, then follows up while the enemy is disrupted.

orc_warchief.name: orc warchief
orc_warchief.lore: An orc warchief commands multiple warbands and holds territory through sheer personal power. They are chosen not by birth but by combat — any orc can challenge a warchief, though few survive the attempt. Their strategems are surprisingly sophisticated for creatures who view battlefield elegance as weakness.

bandit_captain.name: bandit captain
bandit_captain.lore: Bandit captains are experienced fighters who have chosen outlawry over legitimate service. They lead small bands of armed robbers and claim dungeon sections as toll roads. Their combat style is practical and ruthless — they target the most dangerous enemy first and show no mercy.

dark_cultist.name: dark cultist
dark_cultist.lore: Dark cultists have abandoned civilized life to serve entities of shadow and corruption. They know enough ritual magic to be dangerous — their prayers can manifest as shadowy attacks. They seek artifacts of power for their patron and will sacrifice anything, including themselves, for their cause.

mercenary_veteran.name: mercenary veteran
mercenary_veteran.lore: Mercenary veterans are former soldiers who have survived too many campaigns. They have no political allegiance — they serve whoever pays, currently some dungeon power. Their combat technique is honed by decades of professional violence, making them far more dangerous than their simple equipment suggests.

battle_mage.name: battle mage
battle_mage.lore: Battle mages specialize in combat-oriented magic, sacrificing breadth of spellcasting for power and speed. They typically wear light armor and rely on keeping distance from melee. The arcane tattoos covering their arms are magical conduits that accelerate spellcasting at the cost of significant pain.

hill_giant.name: hill giant
hill_giant.lore: Hill giants are the most common giant variety, dull-witted but immensely strong. They build crude dens in cavern systems and eat anything they can catch, including other giants if sufficiently hungry. Their hoards are eclectic collections of things they found interesting — shiny, large, or edible.

stone_giant.name: stone giant
stone_giant.lore: Stone giants dwell in the deepest caverns, their gray skin nearly indistinguishable from dungeon walls. They are far more intelligent than hill giants and have developed a sophisticated culture centered on art carved into cave walls. They view the surface world with contempt and surface-dwellers who venture below as trespassers.

cloud_giant.name: cloud giant
cloud_giant.lore: Cloud giants float between the material and elemental planes, their castles built on impossible storm-clouds. Those found in the deep dungeon are typically exiles or adventurers of their own kind, seeking ancient artifacts. They are proud to the point of arrogance, finding most humanoids too small to be worth killing.

storm_giant.name: storm giant
storm_giant.lore: Storm giants are among the most powerful mortal beings in existence, their power rivaling minor deities. They perceive the world on a geological timescale — they remember when the dungeon was first built. Their purposes are inscrutable to shorter-lived beings, but when they act, the consequences are felt for generations.

cyclops_brute.name: cyclops brute
cyclops_brute.lore: Cyclops brutes are larger and more violent relatives of the mythological cyclops, stripped of any shepherd's gentleness. Their single eye cannot gauge depth well, making them poor archers but devastating in melee where they can feel their target. They collect sheep-sized livestock or humanoids with equal indifference.

two_headed_ogre.name: two-headed ogre
two_headed_ogre.lore: Two-headed ogres are the result of a rare magical mutation — or perhaps a divine punishment. Their two heads argue constantly about tactics, food, and everything else, but in combat their four-armed style of attack is brutally effective. They can attack two targets simultaneously, which makes fighting them with a partner especially hazardous.

ice_troll.name: ice troll
ice_troll.lore: Ice trolls haunt the frozen sub-levels and flooded caverns that connect to glacial systems. Their regeneration is slowed by fire and stopped completely by salt, but in their icy domains they are nearly unkillable. Their saliva freezes on contact with warm flesh.

drow_warrior.name: drow warrior
drow_warrior.lore: Drow warriors are trained from birth in the fighting arts of the Underdark. Their equipment is of exquisite quality, though much of it crumbles in surface air. They favor poison-laced crossbow bolts to disable opponents for capture — drow prefer prisoners over corpses when possible.

drow_mage.name: drow mage
drow_mage.lore: Drow mages are among the most accomplished arcane practitioners in the deep dungeon. They specialize in shadow magic and illusions, disciplines that evolved in a lightless environment where shadows have more reality than light. Their spellbooks, if recoverable, are worth significant sums to surface scholars.

drow_priestess.name: drow priestess
drow_priestess.lore: Drow priestesses serve the Spider Queen and hold absolute authority in drow society above all but the Matron Mothers. They wield divine magic of terrifying efficacy, particularly spells of darkness, poison, and domination. Their favor raises the status of entire families; their displeasure ends bloodlines.

minotaur_warrior.name: minotaur warrior
minotaur_warrior.lore: Minotaur warriors are organized fighters who have built a martial culture around labyrinth warfare. They never become disoriented in maze-like environments and use this advantage ruthlessly. A minotaur warrior in its home territory is three times as dangerous as one encountered in open ground.

young_black_dragon.name: young black dragon
young_black_dragon.lore: Black dragons are among the most vile of dragonkind — cruel, sadistic, and petty. They lair in swamps and flooded dungeon sections, where their acid breath is most effective. Even young, they are extraordinarily dangerous and already beginning to plot the accumulation of a hoard worthy of their ego.

young_red_dragon.name: young red dragon
young_red_dragon.lore: Red dragons are the most arrogant of dragonkind, believing themselves superior to all other beings. Even a young red dragon has a fully developed ego and expects tribute. Their fire breath is the hottest of all dragon breath weapons — it can melt stone if sustained. Red dragon lairs are always notably warmer than surrounding areas.

adult_blue_dragon.name: adult blue dragon
adult_blue_dragon.lore: Adult blue dragons are fascinated by organization and order — not law in a moral sense, but perfect systemic arrangement. They maintain extensive networks of humanoid servants and are excellent strategists. Their lightning breath can chain between multiple targets, making them especially deadly against groups.

adult_red_dragon.name: adult red dragon
adult_red_dragon.lore: Adult red dragons are among the most feared creatures in existence. Their personality is a perfect storm of greed, pride, and cruelty. An adult red dragon's hoard is legendary — accumulated over centuries of systematic raiding and tribute-taking. They consider themselves the rightful rulers of whatever territory they claim.

drake_fire.name: fire drake
drake_fire.lore: Fire drakes are lesser kin to true dragons, possessing a fraction of their cousin's power and none of their intelligence. They operate on instinct — hunt, eat, hoard. Their fire spit is considerably less devastating than a dragon's breath but sufficient to ignite most dungeon materials. They are sometimes found as guards in more powerful monsters' lairs.

drake_cold.name: frost drake
drake_cold.lore: Frost drakes haunt the glacier-touched lower dungeon levels, their scales perpetually rime-covered. They ambush from above, coating prey with freezing breath before descending to feed. Their lairs are always recognizable by the icy coating on surrounding walls and the frozen remains of previous meals.

yuan_ti_pureblood.name: yuan-ti pureblood
yuan_ti_pureblood.lore: Yuan-ti are serpent-human hybrids who worship a dead serpent god whose demise did not diminish their devotion. Purebloods appear almost entirely human, their serpentine nature revealed only by slightly scaled patches of skin. They infiltrate human settlements and dungeon exploration parties as spies for the yuan-ti collective.

wyvern_greater.name: greater wyvern
wyvern_greater.lore: Greater wyverns are among the largest flying predators in the dungeon, their wingspan capable of blocking corridor exits. Unlike true dragons, they have no fire breath — they rely on their savage claws and an enormously powerful stinger at the tail's tip. Their venom is among the most potent naturally occurring toxins.

naga_guardian.name: naga guardian
naga_guardian.lore: Nagas are powerful serpentine beings of vast intelligence and magical ability. Guardian nagas in particular have been assigned permanent watch over specific locations — tombs, vaults, shrines — and have carried out this duty for centuries. They know everything about their assigned territory and find intruders personally offensive.

shadow_master.name: shadow master
shadow_master.lore: Shadow masters are Greater Shadows that have developed distinct consciousness through centuries of draining life force. They retain a distorted memory of their former lives, which makes them far more cunning than common shadows. They move through darkness as through water and prefer to attack from behind.

void_crawler.name: void crawler
void_crawler.lore: Void crawlers emerge from tears between dimensions, drawn to the concentrated magical energy of deep dungeons. Their bodies are partially phased out of reality, making conventional attacks less effective. Their touch induces vivid hallucinations as a byproduct of the dimensional interference their presence creates.

ruinous_djinn.name: ruinous djinn
ruinous_djinn.lore: Ruinous djinn are genies whose imprisonment drove them to madness. Freed at last but unable to return home, they destroy as a reflex — every wall they could not pass, every chain they could not break, they now break in others. Their power is undiminished; their purpose is simply wrath.

undead_colossus.name: undead colossus
undead_colossus.lore: Undead colossi are constructed from the remains of giants, assembled by necromancers who required a servant capable of bashing through stone walls. The stitching and binding runes are crudely visible — these were meant to be disposable weapons, not prestige projects. They do not think; they only obey the last command given.

elder_werewolf.name: elder werewolf
elder_werewolf.lore: Elder werewolves have survived decades of transformations, their human side now permanently subordinate to the beast. They lead packs of younger werewolves, coordinating hunts with tactical intelligence no ordinary wolf could manage. Their bite carries a particularly aggressive strain of lycanthropy, and they are cunning enough to aim for joints and tendons to cripple before killing.

abyssal_champion.name: abyssal champion
abyssal_champion.lore: Abyssal champions are demons who have earned rank through conquest — each one has slain a thousand mortal warriors and has the trophies to prove it. They seek out skilled fighters specifically, needing worthy opponents to justify their title. Defeating an abyssal champion is considered a mark of extraordinary martial achievement.

bone_titan.name: bone titan
bone_titan.lore: Bone titans dwarf ordinary bone golems the way a castle dwarfs a cottage. Assembled from the skeletons of multiple giants, they stand thirty feet tall and their footsteps shake dungeon floors. Their mass alone makes them devastating; the necromancer who builds one typically keeps it for defense of an entire dungeon wing, not a single room.

spectral_lord.name: spectral lord
spectral_lord.lore: Spectral lords are the ghosts of powerful mages or warlords whose ambition kept them from passing on. They have had decades or centuries to grow in power, their incorporeal form accumulating magical energy like a storm accumulates lightning. They retain their intelligence and their contempt for the living.

chaos_troll.name: chaos troll
chaos_troll.lore: Chaos trolls have been mutated by proximity to void energy, their regenerative capacity accelerated to grotesque extremes. They regrow severed limbs mid-battle, sometimes growing back wrong — extra arms, heads facing the wrong direction. Fire is the only reliable way to stop their regeneration. Anything else just makes them angrier.

wyrm.name: wyrm
wyrm.lore: Wyrms are dragons that have not yet achieved true flight — their wings too underdeveloped to lift their growing bulk. They compensate with ground speed and breath weapon intensity. Many young dragons pass through a wyrm phase between juvenile and adult, and these creatures are especially dangerous, caught between caution and arrogance.

elder_mind_flayer.name: elder mind flayer
elder_mind_flayer.lore: Elder mind flayers have fed on exceptional intellects for centuries, absorbing knowledge with each brain consumed. They are vastly more powerful than common illithids, their psionic blasts capable of erasing short-term memory entirely. They view combat as an indignity — by the time they are forced to fight, they are already furious.

void_leviathan.name: void leviathan
void_leviathan.lore: Void leviathans are titanic serpentine creatures from the space between dimensions, drawn through by rifts created by powerful magic. Their bodies partially phase in and out of reality as they move, which makes them nearly impossible to bind or contain. They consume anything that generates heat — including living creatures.

elder_beholder.name: elder beholder
elder_beholder.lore: Elder beholders are ordinary beholders who have survived long enough to develop additional eye stalks and master their anti-magic eye to near-perfection. They are the apex predators of the dungeon's middle depths, their intelligence bordering on supernatural. They maintain detailed records of every creature they have encountered, categorized by threat level and edibility.

dread_wyrm.name: dread wyrm
dread_wyrm.lore: Dread wyrms are elder wyrms whose growth has been stunted by magical corruption, preventing them from becoming true adult dragons. Trapped between forms, they fester with resentment, their fire hotter and their temperament worse than any proper dragon. They are easy to provoke and impossible to negotiate with — a bad combination at any dungeon depth.

abyssal_overlord.name: abyssal overlord
abyssal_overlord.lore: Abyssal overlords command entire layers of the Abyss — vast planes of pure chaos where millions of lesser demons fight and die without purpose. When an overlord descends to the material plane, it brings a fragment of that chaos with it. Reality itself becomes unstable within a hundred yards of their presence.

elder_dragon.name: elder dragon
elder_dragon.lore: Elder dragons occupy the threshold between adult and ancient — creatures who have lived for five centuries and accumulated enough power to reshape landscapes, but who have not yet developed the full magical mastery of their elders. They are at their most dangerous here, aggressive enough to destroy anything that threatens their hoard but wise enough to choose their battles.

lich_sovereign.name: lich sovereign
lich_sovereign.lore: Lich sovereigns once ruled kingdoms in life and refuse to relinquish that authority in undeath. They govern their dungeon domains with the same bureaucratic thoroughness they applied to their kingdoms, maintaining records of every intruder and detailed plans for the disposal of each. Their phylacteries are hidden in the ruins of their mortal capitals.

demon_emperor.name: demon emperor
demon_emperor.lore: Demon emperors rule through the combination of overwhelming power and complex political maneuvering. They maintain their position through constant warfare, both against mortal realms and rival demon lords. Every demon emperor has personally destroyed at least one civilization, which they count as a prerequisite for the title rather than a crime.

world_serpent.name: world serpent
world_serpent.lore: World serpents are fragments of the mythic cosmic serpent said to encircle the plane itself. How they came to be confined in dungeons is unknown — perhaps they were summoned centuries ago and simply stayed when the summoner died, or perhaps they are drawn to the deep places where the world's foundations are exposed. They are older than most civilizations.

entropy_wraith.name: entropy wraith
entropy_wraith.lore: Entropy wraiths are the inevitable end of sufficiently old undead spirits — wraiths that have existed so long they have begun dissolving the boundary between themselves and nothingness. They are not malicious; they simply are, and their presence unmakes. The void that follows them is not death but something older: the absence that preceded existence.

asterion_minotaur.name: Asterion the Minotaur
asterion_minotaur.lore: Asterion, son of Pasiphae and the Cretan Bull, imprisoned in the Labyrinth by King Minos. Cursed with a bestial form, he hungers endlessly and has claimed many young lives. His horns can gore through plate armor.

medusa_gorgon.name: Medusa the Gorgon
medusa_gorgon.lore: Once a beautiful mortal priestess, transformed by Athena's curse into a creature whose gaze turns men to stone. Her snake-hair strikes independently, and even her blood holds deadly magic. Only a mirror shield can safely meet her gaze.

fafnir_dragon.name: Fafnir the Dragon
fafnir_dragon.lore: The dwarf Fafnir, transformed by greed into a serpentine dragon, guards the cursed treasure of Andvari. His scales are near-impenetrable, his breath weapon devastating. Only the hero Sigurd, guided by the wisdom of birds, could slay him.

fenrir_wolf.name: Fenrir, Wolf of Ragnarok
fenrir_wolf.lore: Son of Loki, the great wolf was prophesied to devour Odin at Ragnarok. Bound by Gleipnir, he tore off the hand of Tyr. Now freed as the age of gods ends, he is the most terrible beast in all the Nine Realms.

abaddon_destroyer.name: Abaddon the Destroyer
abaddon_destroyer.lore: The Angel of the Bottomless Pit, called Destruction. He who has dominion over the locusts of the Abyss. King of the fallen host, bearer of the key to the pit. His arrival signals the end and his defeat, the dawn of wisdom's triumph.

arachne.name: Arachne
arachne.lore: Once a mortal weaver of unmatched skill, Arachne challenged Athena herself to a contest and was transformed into a spider as punishment. Her web can hold a man fast, and her bite carries venom.

talos.name: Talos
talos.lore: Talos was a bronze automaton forged by Hephaestus to guard the island of Crete, circling its shores three times daily. His single vein of ichor runs from neck to ankle — drain that life-fluid and he falls.

echidna.name: Echidna
echidna.lore: Echidna, half-woman half-serpent, is the mother of Cerberus, the Hydra, the Chimera, and the Sphinx. She dwells in a cave and never ages, birthing monsters into the world without end.

erlking.name: Erlking
erlking.lore: The Erlking rides the wind through dark forests, luring travelers — especially children — to their doom with beautiful music and false promises. Iron and flame alone disturb his reverie.

camazotz.name: Camazotz
camazotz.lore: Camazotz is the Mayan lord of bats and the night, dwelling in Xibalba, the underworld. He is said to have decapitated the Hero Twin Hunahpu. His wings blot out all light, and his bite drains the soul.

cacus.name: Cacus
cacus.lore: Cacus was a fire-breathing giant, son of Vulcan, who terrorized the countryside until Hercules slew him for stealing the cattle of Geryon. He drags prey backward into his cave to hide his tracks.

the_sphinx.name: The Sphinx
the_sphinx.lore: The Sphinx crouches at the gates between realms, devouring those who cannot answer her riddle. She is cunning above all other beasts, and her patience is absolute. Only wisdom disarms her.

rangda.name: Rangda
rangda.lore: Rangda is the demon queen of Bali, mistress of witches and widow of chaos. She leads Leyaks — black magic users — in battle against the divine Barong. Her howl can shatter a warrior’s will.

nemean_lion.name: Nemean Lion
nemean_lion.lore: The Nemean Lion's hide was impervious to all weapons — no blade or arrow could pierce it. Hercules strangled the beast with his bare hands and used its own claws to skin it. Only blunt force can harm it.

baba_yaga.name: Baba Yaga
baba_yaga.lore: Baba Yaga lives in a hut on chicken legs deep in an enchanted forest. Her iron teeth can chew through armor as through bread. She tests heroes who find her — those who fail her trials are eaten.

jormungandr_juvenile.name: Jörmungandr (Juvenile)
jormungandr_juvenile.lore: The World Serpent Jörmungandr encircles all of Midgard, and even in its juvenile form it carries the venom that will one day slay Thor at Ragnarök. Its poison is said to be unkillable by any remedy.

sets_jackal.name: Set's Jackal
sets_jackal.lore: A jackal-headed enforcer of Set, god of chaos and storms. Where Anubis guides the dead justly, Set's jackals hunt the living for sport. Their touch drains vitality and plunges the world into shadow.

green_knight.name: The Green Knight
green_knight.lore: The Green Knight cannot be permanently killed — his head rolls and he picks it up again. He offered his axe to Gawain for one blow, knowing he would return the favor a year hence. His endurance is legendary.

charybdis.name: Charybdis
charybdis.lore: Charybdis swallows the sea three times a day, creating whirlpools that drag ships to their doom. Only the great Odysseus survived her by clinging to a fig tree above her maw. She draws everything toward her.

ravanas_arm.name: Ravana's Arm
ravanas_arm.lore: Ravana, the ten-headed demon king of Lanka, lost an arm to Rama's divine bow. The arm crawls on its own, animated by Ravana's immense life-force, striking with all the fury of its master's wrath.

wendigo.name: Wendigo
wendigo.lore: The Wendigo is born when a human resorts to cannibalism in the frozen north — the ice spirit possesses them and drives them to hunger without end. It drains warmth and satiety, leaving only frozen despair.

wild_hunt_captain.name: Wild Hunt Captain
wild_hunt_captain.lore: The Wild Hunt Captain leads the spectral riders who sweep across the winter sky, collecting the souls of the living. To hear the horn is to be marked for death. Iron alone can slow their phantom charge.

anansi.name: Anansi
anansi.lore: Anansi is the spider god who owns all stories. He won tales from the Sky God Nyame by trapping Onini the python and Osebo the leopard with his cleverness alone. He never fights directly — he tricks.

nidhoggr_fragment.name: Níðhöggr Fragment
nidhoggr_fragment.lore: Níðhöggr gnaws at the roots of Yggdrasil, the World Tree, working to unravel all of creation. Even a fragment of his body carries his relentless hunger and his acid venom. He will outlast everything.
